programme.title: Father Dowling Mysteries
programme.desc: Sister Steve poses as a croupier to help the crime-buster expose a robbery and solve the murder of an exotic dancer. Tom Bosley and Tracy Nelson star

programme.title: Father Dowling Mysteries
programme.desc: Sister Steve's tearaway brother becomes involved in a gang war and Father Dowling has the misfortune to find himself caught in the crossfire. Drama, starring Tom Bosley and Tracy Nelson

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Kimble is befriended by a woman who has fled city life and a man who has fled civilisation

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: When the $200,000 she finds in a shoebox disappears, a young woman asks Perry Mason for advice

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Adventure with the modern-day lawman whose crime-solving methods are rooted firmly in the Old West. Chuck Norris stars

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: Horatio hunts for the mystery assailant responsible for an attack on a woman who was lured to a vacant condominium and beaten with a piece of timber. The investigation leads him to a Georgia prison - and prompts a surprising confession from Delko. David Caruso, Emily Procter and Adam Rodriguez star

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs sends DiNozzo and Ziva to retrieve top-secret information from a secure military base - a mission aimed at catching a mole in the NCIS ranks. Unfortunately, it soon becomes clear that the traitor is still at large - so the building is locked down and all agents are rounded up in a final effort to catch the culprit. Guest starring Jude Ciccolella

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Part one of two. The discovery that a murder weapon used in Idaho matches one used in a national security case back in LA brings Sam and Callen into contact with NCIS: Red, a mobile team of agents who live and work together solving crimes across the US - and soon they are signed up as honorary members to help piece together the puzzle. Kim Raver (24) and John Corbett (Sex and the City) guest star

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: The running finally comes to an end as the mystery of the death of Richard Kimble's wife is fully unravelled

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: An oilman asks Perry Mason to handle a transaction involving the sale of oil leases at a surprisingly low price

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and his team investigate the bizarre deaths of a marine and two of his friends at a stag party. Elsewhere, Gibbs tries to persuade Vance to allow Ziva back into the NCIS. However, she must first pass a barrage of psychological evaluations. Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette star

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Adventure with the modern-day lawman whose crime-solving methods are rooted firmly in the Old West. Chuck Norris stars

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A marine lieutenant's body falls from the sky and the team links the man's death to the disappearance of a Navy chaplain thought to have been kidnapped by rebels working with Colombian crime cartels. Ziva and Tony are dispatched to conduct a rescue operation and enlist the help of former Interpol agent Monique Lisson, while Gibbs discovers there was more to the chaplain's outreach mission than meets the eye. Crime drama, guest starring Karina Lombard (The L Word) and Jamie Lee Curtis (A Fish Called Wanda)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Part two of two. The LA and Red agents continue their collaborative effort to track down the elusive Robert Spears, with intelligence from captured terrorist Sayed leading them to El Centro in Mexico. Kim Raver (24) and John Corbett (Sex and the City) guest star with Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A blogger who accused the NCIS of a cover-up is found dead, and Gibbs and his team are forced to reopen a closed case to find the killer. Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Cote de Pablo, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette star

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Part two of two. Mark recalls an incident from 20 years previously that appears to hold the key to solving the case involving cover-ups, radioactive fuel and the death of an airline rep. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A TV executive is murdered and the subsequent death of the chief suspect makes Mark and Steve realise a serial killer is on the loose. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team members are determined to prove their worth after they fail a military training exercise ordered by Director Merrick. Meanwhile, random bomb strikes threaten the internet infrastructure around the city, and Scorpion sets up an operation to find the perpetrator. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team members go against Cabe's advice and travel to Las Vegas for what they believe will be a simple job helping the owners of a casino. However, Walter is suspected of performing a heist and is arrested, so Scorpion is left to clear his name. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part two of two. DiNozzo, McGee and the FBI continue to track political connections to a case, and Bishop sends DiNozzo a souvenir. Meanwhile, Gibbs and Special Agent Pride chase leads in New Orleans after evidence points to a copycat killer. Starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Pauley Perrette and Scott Bakula

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: McGee's girlfriend Delilah makes a breakthrough in a case involving the alleged death of a US navy lieutentant. However, when her boss orders her to back away, she turns to the NCIS team for help. Meanwhile, McGee talks to DiNozzo about whether he should move forward with his relationship. Crime drama, starring Sean Murray

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team must ascertain whether the murder of a Navy officer, whose body was found on a rural road, was the result of a bar fight or because he knew too much about an attack on a fellow female officer. Meanwhile, DiNozzo and McGee begin a juice detox to purge their bodies of impurities. Starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Cote de Pablo and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the disappearance of photographer Martin Roe, who was due to testify at an army first lieutenant's court-martial after he captured a picture of what appeared to be a soldier killing an unarmed civilian. However, the photograph is leaked, and Roe is later found dead with a needle in his arm

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Director Vance charges DiNozzo with a special assignment to go to France, locate Admiral Kendall's wayward daughter Amanda and bring her home. Arriving in Marseille, Tony immediately heads to the local NCIS office, only to find five dead agents. After eventually tracking down Amanda, the pair embark on a rollercoaster ride to the relative safety of the nearest NCIS office in Spain. Michael Weatherly stars

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Chief petty officer Leland Wiley dies of a heart attack just as he is about to reveal information on elusive arms dealer Agah Bayar. The team discovers the cardiac arrest was caused by someone hacking into a computer monitoring Wiley's pacemaker and increasing his heart rate to 400bpm. Meanwhile, a probationary agent tries to impress Gibbs and win a place on his team. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon, with Tamer Hassan and Matt L Jones

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Confidential files are leaked by an anonymous hacker, who threatens to release more unless the Navy agrees to renew a contract with a struggling shipbuilding firm. The Navy Secretary suspects the company's owner Philip Wickes is to blame, and asks NCIS to join forces with the PsyOps unit in an elaborate ruse to bring the culprit out of hiding. Meanwhile, Gibbs' colleagues begin to notice his attraction to his opposite number Samantha Ryan. Guest starring Jamie Lee Curtis

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Vance's brother-in-law Michael Thomas is linked to the murder of a petty officer, and Gibbs is placed under pressure to clear the man's name. Meanwhile, a negative performance review leaves Tony feeling downhearted and he resolves to prove the assessment wrong by being less chatty while on duty. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Rocky Carroll

programme.title: The Heart Guy
programme.desc: Ajax takes desperate measures to stop Hugh selling the farm, Penny discovers her perfect match thanks to Meryl's dating service, and Matt gets drunk with April

With Philip Glenister and Marc Warren

programme.desc: In a bid to liven up the camp, Joe Maplin decrees a weekly Sunshine Smile Day - giving perennial wide-boy Ted the perfect opportunity to fleece unsuspecting holiday-makers out of their spare cash. Comedy, starring Paul Shane

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Richard has an accident and Hyacinth decides to buy a small country retreat - but her plans go awry in a most unexpected fashion. Comedy, starring Patricia Routledge and Clive Swift

The net finally closes in on Kevin and Ashley - but Tommy is still at large. Sarah Lancashire, Steve Pemberton and Siobhan Finneran star

The case also helps Gerry to face up to his relationship with Emily - who has not spoken to him since she discovered the truth about the DNA test at Ricky Hanson's trial. Police drama, starring Alun Armstrong and Amanda Redman

programme.title: Taggart

Detective drama, guest starring Gordon Kennedy and Joanna Roth

programme.title: The Heart Guy
programme.desc: When he learns the identity of the new owner, Hugh tries to stop the farm sale, Matt is torn between his feelings for Charlie and April, and Meryl uncovers council corruption

programme.desc: Charlie Gimbert plans an elaborate double-cross to get his hands on a pair of valuable Meissen figurines, sold to Lovejoy by a woman who needed some quick cash to buy drugs. Drama, starring Ian McShane

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: A robbery at the local museum sets Lovejoy off on the trail of a hoard of Roman coins hidden on the Isle of Man - but he soon discovers two Americans are also searching for it. Drama, starring Ian McShane

programme.title: Lovejoy

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: When a man who arranged to meet Lovejoy at the local cricket game dies suddenly, it falls to the antiques dealer to find the fortune that was promised to the widow. The unusual legacy includes a forged snuff box. Ian McShane stars

programme.desc: A movie crew from Hollywood arrives in the village to make a film, and pesky pensioners Compo, Clegg and Foggy manage to get themselves roped in as extras. Norman Wisdom guest stars, with Bill Owen, Peter Sallis and Brian Wilde

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: Laura and Rosemary are hired to create a restaurant garden on Italy's Ligurian coast, but it is not long before their working holiday is disrupted by a murder. Determined to solve the crime before returning home, they find themselves with no shortage of suspects - and matters are complicated further by a second death. Guest starring Suzanne Bertish (Rome) and Tristan Gemmill (Casualty), with Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: An archaeologist posing as Rosemary dies mysteriously during a dig for Anglo-Saxon artefacts in the grounds of Lord Engleton's stately home, presenting the duo with a fresh mystery to solve. The gardens are unaccountably blighted by yellowing, forcing the estate to close its gates to the public, and a second death suggests somebody will go to any lengths for a piece of British history. Guest starring Julian Wadham

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: Laura and Rosemary are called in to investigate when gardens entered for a village contest are attacked by a disease. Further sinister undercurrents are exposed when reigning champion Donald Westward is found dead and recriminations start to fly as buried rivalries come to the surface. Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris star

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: A groom-to-be is shot dead while on a hunting excursion during his stag trip. Though apparently an accident, none of his accompanying friends will admit to firing the bullet. Neill Rea stars

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: Frank and Lu investigate a missing person case at a casino, where gambling is a matter of life or death. Starring Mark Benton and Jo Joyner

programme.title: Rebus
programme.desc: The detective angers his superiors when he tries to connect a member of Edinburgh's criminal fraternity to the shooting of two boys and a teacher at a local college. One of the victims turns out to be the son of Rebus's cousin, giving Templar the perfect excuse to take him off the case. However, the copper is determined to trace a schoolgirl he is certain holds the key to the mystery. Ken Stott stars

programme.title: Wallander
programme.desc: An old friend asks the detective to investigate the death of his father, which he is convinced should be treated as murder. programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Izzie Balmer hit the shops of Staffordshire and Shropshire. Izzie's new obsession turns out to be serpentine in nature as she gets her hands on a couple of snake-related items. For Phil, there's a rather special bit of jewellery from a big-name designer that he has his sights on

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating present the magazine show, featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Bobby arrives at the restaurant for another date with Dana, only for Iqra to drop him in it by suggesting that he should cook them dinner. Rainie tells Vi to go back home - only to be caught off guard when the old busybody lets her in on a secret. Nancy encourages Mick to speak up about Linda's plan to get in touch with Max about the baby. Isaac continues digging for information on Paul and Callum digs himself deeper as Ben makes a discovery

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Sara Davies are joined by former Dragon Theo Paphitis, who standing in for a self-isolating Peter Jones. The Dragons are served a selection of premium spiced rums by a confident drinks entrepreneur. A nursing bra designed to help mothers and babies breastfeed effectively and a heatless hair curling device invented in a garden shed are also offered up, while a Midlands-based duo pitch a voice-enhanced app for learner-readers. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Launch Show
programme.desc: Gary Lineker and guests look ahead to this summer's European Championship, which begins in Rome with the curtain-raiser between Turkey and Italy. The tournament, which was delayed by a year because of the coronavirus pandemic, will take place in 11 different cities across the continent, with the semi-finals and final staged at London's Wembley Stadium. England have only ever won one knockout tie at the Euros, but hopes are high Gareth Southgate's side can improve that record. The Three Lions, who boast a formidable array of attacking options, begin their campaign against Croatia on Sunday. Scotland meet the Czech Republic in their opener, while Wales face Switzerland. Gary is joined by former England defender Micah Richards, ex-Scotland star James McFadden and Ashley Williams, who captained Wales at Euro 2016

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents an hour of topical debate with a virtual audience from around the UK. On the panel are Minister for Apprenticeships and Skills Gillian Keegan MP, Shadow Secretary of State for Housing Lucy Powell MP, Greek politician and economist Yanis Varoufakis, entrepreneur and founder of an IT company specialising in healthcare Kavita Oberoi and political commentator Frank Luntz

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Adam Fleming, Laura Kuenssberg and Chris Mason present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: Our Cops in the North
programme.desc: A fire that kills a man and could have led to more deaths appears to be arson and the culprit is likely to strike again - but identifying the criminal proves tricky as all the evidence was destroyed in the blaze. Meanwhile, a neighbourhood team gathers enough intelligence to raid the address of a local suspected drug dealer, and paramedics are prevented from carrying out CPR

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell is in her native Scotland looking round a flat in Dumfries, as well as visiting a plot of land in Birmingham, and Martin Roberts is at a house in Abergavenny

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: Luke and Carl visit a north London bar in search of money owed to a company who repaired their equipment but allegedly didn't pay for the work. James and Mark meet a tricky debtor at a car lot who makes extraordinary demands, while Ben and Myles go in search of money believed to be owed by a cleaning company to a former employee

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: A plastic bag heat sealer that convicted an online drug trafficker. Plus, a casual chat with a criminal and a map join the dots to catch three burglars terrorising a neighbourhood

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion looks back at a selection of childhood antiques and collectables bought by past contestants. Anita Manning learns about the childhood game of marbles, while Natasha Raskin Sharp chats to expert Tim Weeks about collecting toys

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Luca tries to help a patient who is suffering in silence. Medical drama series set in a busy Midlands surgery, with Ross McLaren

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants Jason, Mark, Kiran and Angela vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson is in the Shropshire countryside with a couple of parents who have £575,000 to spend on a home for them and their young son. While in the county, Jules visits the Acton Scott Estate, home to an authentic Victorian working farm, and tries his hand at being a wheelwright's apprentice

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Rajeena and Hardeep have moved to their dream house in Great Glen boasting incredible views. However, its garden is an uninspiring blank canvas with an ugly bank of grass that obscures their vista rather than the traditional garden with a modern twist that they would prefer. The bank needs sorting and they want an entertaining area that capitalises on their view across the fields. A water feature and privacy is also important to the pair. Brothers David and Harry Rich compete with Charlie Dimmock to have their designs accepted to transform the patch on a £3,500 budget

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Izzie Balmer explore the antiques emporia of Lincolnshire and Nottinghamshire. Phil has his eye on a few quality antique staples, as well as one hefty horticultural item. Meanwhile, Izzie finds herself in what could well be her perfect shop, containing lots and lots of her favourite sparkly pieces. A magnificent Elizabethan Country House provides the backdrop for their final face-off

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Turkey v Italy (Kick-off 8.00pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of the opening game of the delayed tournament, a Group A fixture that takes place at Stadio Olimpico in Rome. Due to the worldwide coronavirus pandemic, the competition was postponed for a year, but is now finally set to get under way with this curtain-raiser in the Italian capital. Turkey will be looking for a better showing than 2016, when they finished third in a group also containing Croatia, Spain and Czech Republic, and failed to qualify for the last 16 as one of the best third-placed teams, losing out to Northern Ireland on goal difference. Italy, runners-up in 2012, are the group favourites this time around, with head coach and former Manchester City boss Roberto Mancini leading the Azzurri to a major tournament for the first time, following their failure to qualify for the 2018 World Cup in Russia. That marked the first time in 60 years that the four-time world champions had not appeared at FIFA's prestigious eve

programme.title: Crouchy's Year Late Euros: Live
programme.desc: With the delayed Euros finally on, former England striker Peter Crouch joins forces with Maya Jama and Alex Horne to offer their own unique take on the tournament with celebrity guests, players and highlights. Crouchy also spends some time with the Home Nation players before they head off to compete and expect some challenges and a few surprises along the way during this festival of football and fun

programme.title: Alan Shearer's Euro 96: When Football Came Home
programme.desc: The former England striker looks back at the 1996 European Championship, in which he played a pivotal role in helping the host nation reach the semi-finals. Shearer meets up with teammates in the tournament Paul Gascoigne and Teddy Sheringham, as well as manager Terry Venables, to discuss their memories, while Frank Skinner and David Baddiel discuss the making of their iconic anthem Three Lions

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: Turkey v Italy. Another chance to see the opening game of the delayed tournament, a Group A fixture that took place at Stadio Olimpico in Rome

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Ben Tish and Anna Haugh and special guest Emma Bunton. Helen McGinn joins via video link and picks the wines to go with the studio dishes

programme.title: Trooping the Colour
programme.desc: Live coverage of the pageantry and spectacle of the ceremony to mark the official birthday of Queen Elizabeth II, performed by British and Commonwealth army regiments

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell, Martin Roberts and Dion Dublin examine a two-bedroom flat in Godalming, a house in a terrible state of repair in Telford, and a terraced property in Smethwick

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Ben Cooper and Gary Pe help contestants seek out items of value at the East of England Arena in Peterborough to sell for profit at auction. Plus, presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp shows off an interesting find with a family connection

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Wales v Switzerland (Kick-off 2.00pm). Gabby Logan presents coverage of the opening Group A fixture for both sides, which takes place at Olympic Stadium in Baku, Azerbaijan. Wales are entering their second consecutive European Championship, having never previously qualified before their memorable debut appearance in 2016. They emerged top of a group also containing England, Slovakia and Russia, and defeated Northern Ireland in the last 16 before an unforgettable victory over heavy favourites Belgium in the quarter-finals. Chris Coleman's team eventually succumbed to Cristiano Ronaldo and Portugal in the semi-finals, but their remarkable run to the last four will live long in the memory for their fans. Switzerland have qualified for their fifth European Championship, and will be looking to make it to the knockout rounds for the second consecutive time. They pushed Poland hard in the last 16 in 2016, but eventually lost 5-4 on penalties. Arsenal's Granit Xhaka was the only player to miss

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Denmark v Finland (Kick-off 5.00pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of the opening game of Group B, which takes place at Parken, Copenhagen. The Danes famously won this trophy back in 1992, but have only reached the knockout rounds once since then. They failed to qualify for Euro 2016 in France, and finished third in their group at the 2012 tournament in Poland and Ukraine, despite beating the Netherlands in their opening match. Finland are making their debut at the European Championship, having finished second behind Italy in their qualifying group. Their talismanic striker Teemu Pukki will be hoping to make an impact on the biggest stage, having starred for Norwich City since his arrival in 2018. Micah Richards, Alex Scott and Cesc Fabregas provide analysis, with commentary from Jonathan Pearce and Martin Keown

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present a celebrity edition of the music-based quiz show, with comedian Susan Calman, former footballers John Barnes and Chris Kamara, and DJs Vick Hope, Melvin Odoom and Scott Mills. The pressure's on to name as many hit songs and artists as possible to win £10,000 for their chosen charities

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: A couple from Manchester answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Hosted by Danny Dyer

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as Dr Christian Jessen, Dr Ranj Singh, Matt Richardson, Andrew Maxwell, Katie Thistleton, Michelle Ackerley, Mark Moraghan and Tina Hobley take part in the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Ollie is brought into the ED once again with mysterious symptoms, and Rash becomes suspicious of Rosa's behaviour, leaving him convinced she is deliberately poisoning Ollie. Ethan once again pushes for Fenisha to spend time with Matthew instead of himself, and a construction worker brings her baby to the site, and leaves her in a shed that is about to be demolished

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Highlights
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents highlights of the day's three games - Wales v Switzerland in Group A, and Denmark v Finland and Belgium v Russia in Group B. Wales won their most recent match against the Swiss in 2011, and were hoping to make a winning start to their campaign in Baku. The Russians will have been out for revenge against a star-studded Belgian side, who beat them twice in qualifying

programme.title: Thierry Henry: My France, My Euros
programme.desc: The former France striker and Arsenal's all-time leading scorer looks at European Championship stories, assessing the social and cultural impact football has across the continent. First shown as Henry's home country prepared to host Euro 2016 following November 2015's terror attacks in Paris, he explores how the beautiful game can unify a nation, from the success of the Netherlands in 1988 to Euro 96 in England, when 'football came home'. Contributors include Cesc Fabregas, Peter Schmeichel, Ruud Gullit, Jurgen Klinsmann, Alan Shearer, David Baddiel and Ian Broudie

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: Wales v Switzerland. Another chance to see the opening Group A fixture for both sides, which took place at Olympic Stadium in Baku, Azerbaijan

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and Phil Serrell drive through a downpour in Oxfordshire on the trail of treasures, pinning their hopes on a long-lost saucer and a designer brooch

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: In Ellesmere Port, James wants to turn his neglected garden into a Cornish coastal haven, providing a safe space for his dog and visiting family. Enter Charlie Dimmock and Lee Burkhill with competing designs. Which one will James decide to go with?

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: Angela Scanlon returns with the property makeover show in which cutting-edge virtual reality and visual effects enable people to see what the future of their home could look like, before it is built in reality. In the first edition architects Laura Jane Clark and Julian McIntosh compete to remodel Shelley and Steven's three-bedroom semi-detached home in Bromley, south-east London

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Letterbox
programme.desc: Game show hosted by Mel Giedroyc in which contestants go head-to-head to crack a series of passwords as they attempt to take home the £2,500 prize money

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Katie Jeffryes and Noman Hussein from Essex go up against each other as Paul Martin shows them the tricks of the antiques trade before they face an auction showdown

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Pressure in the kitchen is high as the chefs prepare their fish courses. Paul Croasdale is trying to recreate the elegance of Wimbledon with luxury ingredients such as king crab and scallops. Meanwhile, Phil Carmichael and Nick Brodie are both preparing meals using mackerel which hope they can elevate for the exacting standards of the All England Club

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The entrants representing Wales, returning chef Phil Carmichael and newcomers Paul Croasdale and Nick Brodie, go all out to impress with their main courses. Each chef uses Welsh lamb as the centrepiece of their dish, showcasing a range of techniques and using an array of unusual ingredients to elevate their main courses to banquet level

programme.title: Sicily: Wonder of the Mediterranean
programme.desc: Part one of two. Michael Scott traces the history of Sicily, from the first arrival of ancient Greek settlers 3,000 years ago to the arrival of the Byzantine Empire. He begins his journey on the slopes of Mount Etna, Europe's largest active volcano, and discovers how the Greeks began to settle on Sicily's east coast - planting their olives and vines and building great city states that soon came to rival even Athens itself

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham and Michaela Strachan hope to see the rare turtle dove at Wild Ken Hill in Norfolk, while Gillian Burke gets close to young Manx shearwaters in Northern Ireland and Iolo Williams ducks into the river at Alladale to look for lampreys

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Thomas Plant and Charlie Ross evaluate the public's antiques at the Fleet Air Arm Museum, situated on the Navy's largest aviation base, HMS Heron in Somerset. A surprise discovery by the venue's director pushes the value up on one item, while presenter Paul Martin meets curator Dave Morris, whose pioneering way of working is bringing the exhibits to life in a way never previously imagined

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Comedian Sara Pascoe, news anchor Clive Myrie, and TV presenters Angela Scanlon and Rick Edwards continue the daily test of their general knowledge skills. Hosted by Richard Osman

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's exploration of the West Country continues in south Devon. He arrives in Paignton to investigate an extraordinarily high murder rate in the literature of the 1930s. The Dartmouth Steam Railway conveys him in style to the beautiful home of Agatha Christie, in the company of her great-grandson, James Prichard

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: England V New Zealand. Action from day one of the series-concluding Second Test at Edgbaston. England's last Test at this venue was in 2019, when they lost to Australia by 251 runs, denying them a fifth straight victory here and ending a eight-match unbeaten run

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham and Michaela Strachan investigate how British hedgehogs are coping this spring, and Megan McCubbin joins them live in Norfolk to discuss efforts to save the corncrake. Iolo Williams is in the Scottish Highlands, checking on some very special wildcats, and Gillian Burke has a fishy tale about flapper skates in Northern Ireland

programme.title: The Statue Wars: One Summer in Bristol
programme.desc: On Sunday, June 7, 2020, protestors marching to support the Black Lives Matter movement tore down the statue of slave trader Edward Colston and threw it in Bristol's harbour. Caught in the eye of this storm was Bristol's mayor Marvin Rees, the first directly elected mayor of Black African heritage of a major European city. Born and bred in Bristol and himself a descendant of enslaved people, how would he hold the city together in the face of rising tensions that threaten to explode into violent confrontation?

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and Hugh Dennis run through the main talking points of the last seven days with the support of guests Ed Byrne, Rhys James, Athena Kugblenu, Laura Lexx and Alasdair Beckett-King

programme.title: Festival of Funny: Shorts
programme.desc: King of the court Carl is forced to question his idea of masculinity after an obsession with a mysterious new member of his badminton club spirals out of control. Tom Greaves stars

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Building Britain's Biggest Nuclear Power Station
programme.desc: Following the challenges involved with building the Hinkley Point C in the remote Somerset countryside, the first new nuclear power station in Britain for a generation. This time, the team create an extensive flood defence system to protect the site from a nuclear disaster like the one that befell the Fukushima Daiichi power plant in Japan. Elsewhere, cameras follow specialists as they work to uncover unexploded Second World War bombs that threaten the installation of the 4500-tonne cooling water intake heads

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: Joe Lycett hosts as temperatures drop and winter hits the sewing room, with challenges themed around keeping warm and adding sparkle to the coldest months of the year. Patrick and Esme's Pattern Challenge this week is a man's flannel shirt. The trickiest pattern challenge yet, the sewers must create cuffs and collars, challenging both their construction technique and precise sewing skills

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey through East Anglia, where he discovers the Essex origins of the BBC and joins the Women's Land Army to pick damsons at Tiptree

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham and Michaela Strachan bring the latest dramas from the live nest cameras in Norfolk. Iolo Williams heads to Loch Fleet on Scotland's east coast, hoping to catch ospreys and otters out fishing. Gillian Burke checks out the batty nightlife at WWT Castle Espie, and Megan McCubbin joins conservationists shaping the UK of tomorrow

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Izzie Balmer hit the shops of Staffordshire and Shropshire. Izzie's new obsession turns out to be serpentine in nature as she gets her hands on a couple of snake-related items. For Phil, there's a rather special bit of jewellery from a big-name designer that he has his sights on

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Ben and Shelley's new-build garden in Wing, Buckinghamshire, is sloped, impractical and has a tiny patio. Having travelled extensively across Europe, the couple yearn for a Mediterranean courtyard-style garden to remind them of their adventures abroad. It must also feature a children's play zone for youngsters Eva and Maya. Siblings David and Harry Rich Brothers vie with Arit Anderson to help the pair transform this troublesome space

programme.title: Between the Covers
programme.desc: Sara Cox is joined by David Baddiel, Adjoa Andoh, Phil Davis and Katherine Ryan, who share favourite books as well as reviewing the week's two club picks - The Girl with the Louding Voice by Abi Dare, and Sixteen Horses by Greg Buchanan

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's travels resume in leafy Hertfordshire, where he attempts a canoe slalom course, and visits the estate of Dame Barbara Cartland

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Letterbox
programme.desc: Game show hosted by Mel Giedroyc in which pairs of contestants go head-to-head to crack a series of passwords as they attempt to take home the £2,500 prize money

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Paul Martin hosts the antiques-buying challenge from Sussex, with local lady Roxy O'Connor and Sarah Thomas from the West Midlands taking part

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Chefs Phil Carmichael, Paul Croasdale and Nick Brodie continue the battle to cook at a Taste of Summer banquet celebrating 140 years of the Wimbledon Championships. As each of them prepare their desserts, they will all be out to impress as only two can make it through to tomorrow's Wales final

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining Wales chefs compete for a place in the national finals, preparing their entire menus for judges Oliver Peyton, Matthew Fort, Andi Oliver and guest Dan Bloxham - Wimbledon's Master of Ceremonies responsible for presenting the trophy to the tournament's champions - as the programme continues its tennis-themed series

programme.title: Sicily: Wonder of the Mediterranean
programme.desc: Michael Scott concludes his history of Sicily, telling the story of how the Christian Byzantine Empire was swept aside by a Muslim Arab invasion from North Africa. The island was transformed by new trade networks and agricultural techniques, and in the 19th century was unified with Italy - but the greatest threat to their prosperity then came from within and the rise of the Mafia

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham and Michaela Strachan investigate how British hedgehogs are coping this spring, and Megan McCubbin joins them live in Norfolk to discuss efforts to save the corncrake. Iolo Williams is in the Scottish Highlands, checking on some very special wildcats, and Gillian Burke has a fishy tale about flapper skates in Northern Ireland

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin and the team value antiques at Blackpool Tower Circus, where expert James Lewis discovers a glass bowl that was bought at a car-boot sale. Anita Manning gives her opinion on a diamond and pearl necklace, and Paul investigates the historical use of warhorses

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Angela Scanlon, Clive Myrie, Sara Pascoe and Rick Edwards compete in Double Points Friday, and their scores are tallied from the week resulting in an overall champion being crowned

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo crosses the county line from Devon into Somerset, seeing first-hand how willow farmers sought to overcome the challenge from the production of synthetic plastics during the 1930s and hearing how tourism has spread across the region, especially amongst the young. In Bath, Michael visits the former home of a refugee emperor, whose country was invaded by the Italian dictator Mussolini, and in whose name a religious movement began that now flourishes worldwide. Travelling east into Wiltshire, he reaches the largest training area of the British Army, Salisbury Plain

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: England V New Zealand. Action from day two of the series-concluding Second Test at Edgbaston. The sides had not met here in this format since 1999, when Alex Tudor was player of the match as the hosts prevailed by seven wickets

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham and Michaela Strachan round up this year's nest cameras in Norfolk. Iolo Williams heads to the Moray Firth to check on dolphins, and Gillian Burke encounters basking sharks in Northern Ireland

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At Longmeadow, Monty Don turns his attention to containers, planting them up for drama and summer colour in the jewel garden and showing us how to re-pot citrus plants. In Hampshire, Joe Swift meets the designer of an award-winning contemporary garden to explore the techniques and the plants she has used to seamlessly integrate both house and garden

programme.title: Later - with Jools Holland
programme.desc: Jools chats to comedian Romesh Ranganathan, who chooses his favourite Later moments from the archive. Plus, bandleader, multi-instrumentalist, vocalist and producer Emma-Jean Thackray makes her debut on the show, performing new single Say Something. Van Morrison also makes a return to the programme, sharing a number from his recently released 42nd studio album Latest Record Project Volume 1

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Phantom Thread (2017)
programme.desc: Celebrated British dressmaker Reynolds Woodcock falls in love with Alma, a shy but strong-willed waitress who refuses to conform to his meticulous way of living. Paul Thomas Anderson's Oscar-winning drama, starring Daniel Day-Lewis in his final role before retirement, alongside Vicky Krieps and Lesley Manville

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Starbuck discovers a hidden musical ability that bears a mysterious significance to the final five Cylons, and her tune is found to have a connection with three-year-old human-Cylon hybrid Hera. The president authorises Boomer's release from the Brig so she can stand trial for treason on the Cylon Base Ship. Starring Mary McDonnell, Katee Sackhoff and Jamie Bamber

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: The ship continues to deteriorate and Adama faces the prospect of abandoning the vessel altogether. Sam remains comatose, but his brain activity causes unusual power surges, while Hera's kidnapping serves only to strengthen the belief of those onboard Galactica that the child is a key to humanity's future as well as that of the Cylons

programme.title: Dom Digs In
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood finds out what it takes to fill the UK's freezers with peas, joining the harvest on a Yorkshire farm and helping at the factory where they are bagged and frozen

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: The team builds a den for Cara, who has a sweet tooth and loves to dance

programme.title: The Dog Ate My Homework
programme.desc: Team captains Libby and Emerson battle it out, with help from Saturday Mash Up's Joe Tasker and comedians Vic Cook, Marek Larwood and Sukh Ojla

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: The new sport badge is revealed

programme.title: Deadly Dinosaurs with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve tracks down the deadliest of all dinosaur defenders, meeting the armoured tank that was Nodosaurus and testing whether an Iguanodon had what it took to beat a T. Rex. Plus, thanks to some mega engineering, he unleashes an Ankylosaurus tail-swinging robot

programme.title: Human Universe
programme.desc: Is mankind alone, or are there aliens out there, either waiting to be discovered, or on their way to find Earth? Professor Brian Cox spends this episode asking such questions, and what he discovers may raise a few eyebrows. He begins by exploring the human race's efforts to find neighbours in outer space, including the launch of two golden discs containing a greeting from Earth in the 1970s; they are still travelling and are now the most distant man-made objects from the planet. Brian also meets members of the Search for Extra-Terrestrial Intelligence, who have been monitoring radio signals for 50 years without success, before discussing the ingredients needed to make an intelligent civilisation with astrophysicist Dr Frank Drake

programme.title: Mountain Gorilla
programme.desc: Some of the most vulnerable members of the species struggle to survive, including two orphans whose mothers were killed and a young female who is finding it difficult to deal with unfamiliar emotions. The new 'gorilla king' also has trouble keeping those in his care under control. Narrated by Patrick Stewart

programme.title: Mary Berry's Country House Secrets
programme.desc: Mary visits Scone Palace near Perth, where she is invited to help Lady Mansfield prepare for a special dinner with traditional Scottish reels. She also enjoys salmon fishing on the family's private stretch of the Tay, and the regal pastime of Highland deer stalking with William, the future Earl of Mansfield and the family's long-serving gamekeeper

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham and Michaela Strachan round up this year's nest cameras in Norfolk. Iolo Williams heads to the Moray Firth to check on dolphins, and Gillian Burke encounters basking sharks in Northern Ireland

programme.title: Our Wild Adventures
programme.desc: BBC wildlife presenters reflect on memorable trips to the rainforest, with Chris Packham recalling a visit to Pipeline Road in Panama when he saw a staggering array of birds. Steve Backshall recounts his climb of a sheer sandstone mountain in Venezuela, while Gordon Buchanan talks about his four years living in the rainforest while learning his trade as a cameraman

programme.title: Oklahoma! (1955)
programme.desc: A farm girl is torn between two very different suitors - a charming cowboy wandering through the territory and a brooding farmhand with a dark side to his nature. Meanwhile, another cattle driver has finally raised enough money to marry his sweetheart, only to find he has a rival. Rodgers and Hammerstein's musical Western, with Gordon MacRae, Shirley Jones and Rod Steiger. Songs include Oh What a Beautiful Mornin' and I Cain't Say No

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Celia Imrie narrates a celebration of cinema's finest musicals, featuring archive BBC interviews with some of the genre's biggest stars and directors from Hollywood's golden age of star like Marilyn Monroe, Jane Russell, Gene Kelly and Fred Astaire to the modern-day triumphs of La La Land and The Greatest Showman. The programme also explores the connection between box office and music charts, and looks at how stars like the Beatles, Elton John and Abba saw their songs cross over to record-breaking sensations of the silver screen

programme.title: Great British Photography Challenge
programme.desc: The search for an exciting new name in British photography continues. This week sees the six contenders challenged to capture the character of flowers in rural Herefordshire, the adrenalin of urban free running and impress Rankin with a brand advertising assignment

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: In Blackburn, Anita Manning values a gold charm bracelet and Philip Serrell takes a shine to an unusual globe. Paul Martin finds out about the life of singer Kathleen Ferrier

programme.title: Brian Cox's Adventures in Space and Time
programme.desc: The physicist looks at attempts to answer one of the most profound questions we can ask - are we alone in the universe? With scientists sending space probes to the furthest reaches of our solar system and beyond, the scientific search for alien life has begun and Brian explains why this quest deserves to be taken seriously, and explores the chances of extraterrestrials being found

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: England V New Zealand. Action from day three of the series-concluding Second Test at Edgbaston. England's last Test at this venue was in 2019, when they lost to Australia by 251 runs, denying them a fifth straight victory here and ending a eight-match unbeaten run

programme.title: Trooping the Colour
programme.desc: Huw Edwards presents highlights of this morning's military display from Windsor Castle, where the Colour of the former 2nd Battalion Scots Guards was trooped to mark the Queen's official birthday. With reports by Sonali Shah

programme.title: Phil Collins at the BBC
programme.desc: Archive footage of the singer's performances on BBC programmes from Top of the Pops to Parkinson and The Two Ronnies, as well as the hits that saw him top the charts multiple times here and in the US. Including You Can't Hurry Love, A Groovy Kind of Love, One More Night, I Missed Again and Two Hearts

programme.title: Buster (1988)
programme.desc: Fact-based crime drama about the life of Buster Edwards, a member of the gang responsible for the Great Train Robbery. Going on the run after the notorious heist, he flees to Mexico with his family. However, his homesick wife struggles to adapt to her new life, leaving the outlaw with a difficult choice. Starring Phil Collins, Julie Walters, Sheila Hancock, Larry Lamb and Ralph Brown

programme.title: Kinky Boots (2005)
programme.desc: A man inherits a struggling shoe factory, but salvation arrives when he comes up with a plan for the business to manufacture specialist footwear for drag artists. However, the stunned workforce has to be won over, and the new designs must prove their worth at a high-profile fashion show. Comedy drama, starring Chiwetel Ejiofor, Joel Edgerton, Nick Frost and Linda Bassett

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.desc: Live coverage from Florence, where the third meeting of the 2021 Diamond League is taking place. With the Olympics little more than a month away, some of the world's top athletes will be in Tuscany to test out their form, as well as accruing valuable points in the race to be crowned Diamond League champion. Colombia's Olympic triple jump gold medallist Caterine Ibarguen will be among those competing, and there's a clash of champions in the long jump, where reigning world outdoor champion Malaika Mihambo faces current world indoor champion Ivana Spanovic, who also won Olympic bronze in Rio in 2016

programme.title: A Town Like Alice (1956)
programme.desc: Second World War PoW Jean Paget is sent on a gruelling forced march between Japanese labour camps, but finds comfort in her friendship with Australian captive Joe Harman. During the journey Joe is caught stealing, tied to a cross and left to die. After the conflict is over, Jean is startled to receive a letter postmarked in Joe's home town of Alice Springs. Jack Lee's drama, based on the novel by Nevil Shute, starring Virginia McKenna, Peter Finch, Takagi and Jean Anderson

programme.title: Coventry Cathedral: Building for a New Britain
programme.desc: In November 1940, German bombers destroyed much of the city centre of Coventry and reduced the city's medieval cathedral to rubble. This documentary tells the story of the new cathedral, consecrated on the same site in May 1962 and incorporating the ruins of the old building. Narrated by Pauline Black

programme.title: How to Build a Cathedral
programme.desc: Architectural historian Jon Cannon explores how cathedrals were constructed, looking at fan vaulting at Gloucester, stained glass at York, and the Octagon at Ely for examples of medieval ingenuity, showing how they were built to realise a vision of Heaven on Earth

programme.title: Architects of the Divine: The First Gothic Age
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the development of the Perpendicular style, which took Britain away from the French-inspired buildings of the medieval age. The buildings examined include Gloucester Cathedral, which was built to commemorate a murdered monarch, and King's College Chapel in Cambridge, one of the last buildings to be constructed in the style. Presented by Nina Ramirez

programme.title: Extra Life: A Short History of Living Longer
programme.desc: David Olusoga and Steven Johnson consider how, during a health crisis, the readiness of communities to engage with - and act upon - advice can have a significant impact on the spread of disease

programme.title: Singer-Songwriters at the BBC
programme.desc: A compilation of performances highlighting the growth of the singer-songwriter genre in the 1970s. Featuring songs by Peter Sarstedt, Carole King, Jim Croce, Bridget St John, Cat Stevens, Judy Collins, Randy Newman, John Sebastian, Joan Armatrading, Ralph McTell, Al Stewart, Kevin Coyne, Billy Joel, Tim Hardin and Paul Simon

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Gary Davies presents the edition first aired on February 14, featuring performances by 808 State, the Railway Children, Chris Isaak, Praise, Nomad featuring MC Mikee Freedom, the Simpsons and the Source featuring Candi Staton

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Mark Goodier presents the edition first aired on February 21, featuring performances by Chris Rea, Kenny Thomas, DJH featuring Stefy, Thunder, Xpansions, MC Hammer, Oleta Adams and Living Colour

programme.title: Radio 2 in Concert
programme.desc: One of the most representative and influential bands of the 1980s, Boy George and Culture Club have sold over 150 million records worldwide. Winners of numerous awards, they rose to fame in the early 1980s with an illustrious music career now spanning four decades. This intimate show, recorded at the BBC Radio Theatre in London, features such classic tunes as Karma Chameleon and Do You Really Want to Hurt Me?, as well as songs from Life, their first album in 19 years

programme.title: Boy George and Culture Club: Karma to Calamity
programme.desc: A look at the history of the band, examining its rise to fame, eventual disintegration and the personalities within the group who made it such a success in the early 1980s. Director Mike Nicholls follows the band members as they meet in singer Boy George's London home to write new material following their decision to reunite in 2014

programme.title: Boy George's 1970s: Save Me from Suburbia
programme.desc: The BBC's My Generation season continues as Boy George revisits his teenage years. While history may remember the 1970s as being marked by strikes, poverty and civil discord, the singer reveals that - for the young George O'Dowd - the decade was the beginning of his musical, social and sexual coming-of-age. He discusses how sexual repression and disenfranchisement, along with the advent of glam rock, disco and punk, gave rise to the flamboyant persona he would later unleash on the world, via Culture Club's memorable first appearance on Top of the Pops in 1982

programme.title: Huey Morgan's Latin Music Adventure
programme.desc: After the Communist revolution of 1959, Cuba effectively closed its doors to the sights and sounds of the rest of the world. From this point, its music evolved in isolation from the other Latin speaking countries, with traditional forms placed at the heart of their sound. The rhythms and melodies of Cuba's people have captured the hearts of fans all around the world, and now that the government is gradually relaxing restrictions. Huey sets off from the capital Havana to explore the rich musical legacy of the island, as well as getting a taste of things to come

programme.title: Latin Music: A Session With
programme.desc: The award-winning, classically trained Cuban musician, singer and composer performs music inspired by her Afrocuban heritage. Including tracks from Cubafonia, the album that catapulted her onto the global stage

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Gary Davies presents the edition first aired on February 14, featuring performances by 808 State, the Railway Children, Chris Isaak, Praise, Nomad featuring MC Mikee Freedom, the Simpsons and the Source featuring Candi Staton

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Mark Goodier presents the edition first aired on February 21, featuring performances by Chris Rea, Kenny Thomas, DJH featuring Stefy, Thunder, Xpansions, MC Hammer, Oleta Adams and Living Colour

programme.title: Boy George and Culture Club: Karma to Calamity
programme.desc: A look at the history of the band, examining its rise to fame, eventual disintegration and the personalities within the group who made it such a success in the early 1980s. Director Mike Nicholls follows the band members as they meet in singer Boy George's London home to write new material following their decision to reunite in 2014

programme.title: A Year in the Wild
programme.desc: Exploring 12 months in the lives of people and wildlife inhabiting the New Forest National Park in Hampshire. Although only created in 2005, its woodlands are among the oldest in the country and have supported lifestyles dating back to medieval times. Residents including coppicer Dave Dibden show how their traditions have continued into the present day, and the film also features footage of some of Britain's rarest creatures, including sand lizards, Dartford warblers and fellow birds hobbies. Narrated by Hermione Norris

programme.title: Michael Palin's New Europe
programme.desc: The veteran traveller journeys through Poland from the port of Gdansk in the north to the Slovakian border in the south, visiting Auschwitz, Czestochowa and Warsaw. He explores the historical heritage of the nation, finds time to perform a cameo role with Poland's most famous cabaret group and appears on breakfast TV

programme.title: The Young Montalbano
programme.desc: All preparations for Salvo's move to Genoa have been made, but the inspector himself does not yet seem ready to leave his beloved Sicily. When a young woman is found dead at the bottom of a cliff, having apparently been involved in a tragic car accident, Montelbano is drawn into one last investigation. Augello, Salvo's eager replacement, is quick to accept an easy solution, but his boss remains convinced the inconsistencies need to be examined. Crime drama, starring Michele Riondino. In Italian

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: Documentary examining the use of physically imposed discipline, and how attitudes toward its application have changed over the years. The film investigates the ways schools, religion and the justice system have been involved with corporal punishment, and how its imposition has also provoked debate from a sexual aspect

programme.title: The Killing II
programme.desc: Former detective Sarah Lund is recalled from her low-key job in the countryside by her ex-boss at Copenhagen police HQ after a lawyer is found murdered in macabre and puzzling circumstances. Meanwhile, newly appointed Minister for Justice Thomas Buch oversees intricate negotiations between parliamentary parties concerning the introduction of new anti-terrorism laws. Starring Sofie Grabol and Nicolas Bro. In Danish

programme.title: The Killing II
programme.desc: Lund investigates the possibility of a link between Islamic fundamentalists and the murder of lawyer Anne Dragsholm, which causes Buch problems as he tries to push his anti-terror legislation through parliament. Starring Sofie Grabol and Nicolas Bro. In Danish

programme.title: The Killing II
programme.desc: Raben escapes from prison and sets out in search of a former army comrade. Police arrest their chief suspect, an Islamist activist, but Lund clashes with Strange over the direction of the investigation. Meanwhile, a newly discovered memo puts Buch on the defensive. Crime thriller, starring Sofie Grabol, Ken Vedsegaard and Mikael Birkkjaer. In Danish

programme.title: Gangsta Granny
programme.desc: Family comedy based on the children's novel by David Walliams. Schoolboy Ben is bored witless after being made to stay with his grandma. All she ever wants to do is play Scrabble and eat yucky cabbage soup. But it turns out there's a whole lot more to this OAP than cardigans and toffees when she tells him how she used to be an international jewel thief - and when she admits there was one heist she never quite managed in her heyday, the pair set out on a criminal adventure. Julia McKenzie stars as the shady senior citizen, with Walliams and Miranda Hart as the boys' ballroom-dancing parents, alongside Joanna Lumley, Rob Brydon and Robbie Williams, with Reece Buttery as Ben

programme.desc: Arthur Sleep is joined by team captains Gail Force and Keith Fitt in this slapstick quiz show featuring guests and clips from Gigglebiz. Joining Gail and Keith for this GiggleQuiz are Wizard Tripwick and Captain Adorable. Round 1 features clips starring Gail Force at an archeological dig and the Berrito Brothers testing their muscles with a strength-o-meter. At the end of Round 1, Arthur accidentally drops the Berrito Brother’s massive hammer onto Keith’s buzzer, smashing it into lots of pieces. Always on hand to save the day, Captain Adorable offers to fix the broken buzzer, but after some very unorthodox methods, Keith’s buzzer still isn’t buzzing properly. Round 2 is Nana Knickerbocker’s Panto Showdown, this time featuring Nana’s bees searching for her missing keys that are lost in gloopy, sticky honey. programme.desc: Lucy the helicopter paramedic shows us her helmet. She tells us all about how important her helmet is, and how it helps her to do her job and help people.

programme.title: Andy's Dinosaur Adventures
programme.desc: Andy Day looks at the one of the largest flying pterosaurs, the Ornithocheirus, to help find the skeleton of the Sand Dollar, a sea creature which spent its life on the ocean floor

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie Moate finds out how wearing glasses helps people to see more clearly. She visits an optician to see how an eye test works by having one herself. She reads the charts and shapes on the wall and looks into a special camera that takes a picture of the back of our eyes to make sure they are healthy. Then, Maddie shows us how glasses frames are made by shaping gold wire at a factory. Maddie uses a special microscope camera to show us the ant-sized screws that hold glasses together!

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: The Go Jetters travel to Kinderdijk in the Netherlands to celebrate National Mill Day. A lot of the country is below sea level and there is a high risk of flooding, so at Kinderdijk, ditches and pumps are used to drain away the water

programme.title: YolanDa's Band Jam
programme.desc: YolanDa Brown and her house band The Band Jaminals entertain an audience of children.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Waffle's forgotten to do is omework, and Mr Nolan decides if he can continue as a pupil

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: JoJo & Gran Gran have their own sports day, including an egg and spoon race.

programme.title: Sarah & Duck

programme.title: Twirlywoos

programme.title: Hey Duggee

programme.title: Hey Duggee

programme.desc: Shelly's pink recipe becomes a blobby mess when it is hit by Salty's ball, reminding Jack of the time they encountered a similar looking fish

programme.title: Down on the Farm
programme.desc: Storm Huntley helps plant some lavender, while JB Gill finds out about a spring vegetable and meets some children who are making fresh food on a farm. Plus, a visit a bakery to see how to make pizza

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The squirrels go to see what Duggee is doing, but on the way Tag stumbles on some toy blocks and hurts his knee. How can they make him feel better? Happy's mum always says laughter is the best medicine, so maybe Duggee can help thanks to his comedy badge

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: While walking in the country, Duggee starts to sing a song and marches ahead, while the squirrels march behind him and join in

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: While in Mexico, Glitch takes a short cut through the Cave of Crystals, a hidden space that contains some of the biggest crystals in the world. However, the clumsy Grandmaster falls off one of the crystals and into a deep ravine. It's very hot in the cave and Glitch and the Grimbots are in trouble

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: It's a dark night and Glitch is flying his Grim HQ over the sea around the Isles of Scilly

programme.title: Bitz & Bob
programme.desc: Bitz needs to send two stranded aliens back to space

programme.title: B.O.T and the Beasties
programme.desc: The Happy-Sad Klomp's mood changes with the weather. The Happy-Sad Klom dances when it is sunny and cries when it is cloudy. And when it snows, it is really upset! B.O.T. has to be quick to keep clear of the giant beastie's klompy feet and collect all the data.

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin Fletcher and his friend Lucas visit the Manchester City football ground, and Mr Tumble pitches his football skills against Granddad

programme.title: Maddie, Space and You
programme.desc: Join Maddie as she finds out about the exciting things people do to train to become an astronaut. Maddie explains the different layers of our atmosphere and how astronauts travel through it into space and the special suits they wear to protect them. She tries on a real spacesuit and learns about the types of spacesuits they wear to do different jobs. Maddie discovers astronauts need to be fit, healthy and have good agility. She finds out how they learn how to move in space when they can’t keep their feet on the ground! We discover there is less gravity in space than on Earth and although astronauts look like they are floating, they are free falling.

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: Timmy brings his space hopper to nursery, but the bouncy toy ends up causing lots of accidents, and is confiscated by Harriet

programme.title: Apple Tree House
programme.desc: Determined to make a splash with her cornet solo, Bella practises hard for the community concert

programme.title: Topsy and Tim
programme.desc: The twins celebrate their 5th birthdays with a party, and grandad helps them with their giant balloons

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Evie and Doug enjoy their first playdate together with their friends. They try out their new ball pit, but Waffle cannot join in because he's not toilet trained and he has to find fun elsewhere

programme.title: Andy's Aquatic Adventures
programme.desc: There is a turtle expert visiting the Safari World aquarium. Mr Hammond is on a scuba diving holiday in Borneo, so it’s up to Andy to ensure everything is ready for their arrival

programme.title: Garden Tales
programme.desc: It’s a big day for Grace the Great Tit as the brand new bird feeder cafe is opening for business. She’s spent weeks getting everything ready, and it’s all looking perfect. That is, until Maisie the Grey Squirrel comes and spills seeds everywhere. All of Grace’s hard work is ruined, and all her customers have been scared off. How on earth is she going to get her lovely cafe open again?

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Iona and Elba make pancakes for a fabulous family breakfast. There’s lots of egg cracking, whisking, and fruit chopping, but will Iona manage a successful pancake flip? Later, the sisters build a car for their teddy bear - beep beep! And Iona draws a family tree with Mum and learns all about her relatives, old and new.

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Giovanna and Baby Bear welcome babies Reau, Alfie, Charlie, Ollie, Otis, River and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Parents, carers and their little ones at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too! Inside Giovanna’s bag is a night light, and all the parents and babies have different objects and toys that could be used as part of a bedtime routine. These are explored through discovery, play, song and story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to take part by hiding safe, baby-appropriate bedtime objects and toys inside a fabric bag or pillowcase for them to reveal and explore with their baby! The baby lullaby Little One Time For Bed features, as does a story involving Baby Bear, and viewers at home show what they’ve been doing at bedtime too. The Baby Bear Song gets everyone moving about, before some calm time with sensory lights and the Goodbye Song.

programme.title: Magic Hands
programme.desc: You Are Old, Father William by Lewis Carroll Wynken, Blynken and Nod by Eugene Field Look at what my hands can do! In this two poem episode, Aimee and Ashley are visited by a silvery, slithery eel and lots of twinkling herring fish. Ashley meets Bobbles who takes the role of old Father William to do some impressive back-flips and balance an eel on the end of his nose. Aimee also sails the skies in a wooden shoe with Leapy the Lion, Dixie the Dog and Click Clack the Crab as magical men who are fishing for stars

programme.title: Colours
programme.desc: We learn about objects that are colourful like a rainbow, from chameleons to kites, and explore the deeper meaning of rainbows. The episode concludes with a specially written poem about rainbow colours.

programme.desc: A telling of the Hans Christian Anderson story about an ugly duckling's search to find out who she really is, featuring the Northern Ballet

programme.desc: The youngster and her feathered friend turn detectives when Plate Girl loses her plate on a foggy day

The duo have pooled their resources to have £800,000 to find the right country home where they can live together. Away from the hunt, Alistair is given an exclusive invitation to experience the best in British agriculture at the Royal Three Counties Show

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Ben and Shelley's new-build garden in Wing, Buckinghamshire, is sloped, impractical and has a tiny patio. Having travelled extensively across Europe, the couple yearn for a Mediterranean courtyard-style garden to remind them of their adventures abroad. It must also feature a children's play zone for youngsters Eva and Maya. Siblings David and Harry Rich Brothers vie with Arit Anderson to help the pair transform this troublesome space

Izzie's new obsession turns out to be serpentine in nature as she gets her hands on a couple of snake-related items. For Phil, there's a rather special bit of jewellery from a big-name designer that he has his sights on

programme.title: Pointless

programme.title: The One Show

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Bobby arrives at the restaurant for another date with Dana, only for Iqra to drop him in it by suggesting that he should cook them dinner. Rainie tells Vi to go back home - only to be caught off guard when the old busybody lets her in on a secret. Nancy encourages Mick to speak up about Linda's plan to get in touch with Max about the baby. Isaac continues digging for information on Paul and Callum digs himself deeper as Ben makes a discovery

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Sara Davies are joined by former Dragon Theo Paphitis, who standing in for a self-isolating Peter Jones. The Dragons are served a selection of premium spiced rums by a confident drinks entrepreneur. A nursing bra designed to help mothers and babies breastfeed effectively and a heatless hair curling device invented in a garden shed are also offered up, while a Midlands-based duo pitch a voice-enhanced app for learner-readers. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Launch Show
programme.desc: Gary Lineker and guests look ahead to this summer's European Championship, which begins in Rome with the curtain-raiser between Turkey and Italy. The tournament, which was delayed by a year because of the coronavirus pandemic, will take place in 11 different cities across the continent, with the semi-finals and final staged at London's Wembley Stadium. England have only ever won one knockout tie at the Euros, but hopes are high Gareth Southgate's side can improve that record. The Three Lions, who boast a formidable array of attacking options, begin their campaign against Croatia on Sunday. Scotland meet the Czech Republic in their opener, while Wales face Switzerland. Gary is joined by former England defender Micah Richards, ex-Scotland star James McFadden and Ashley Williams, who captained Wales at Euro 2016

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents an hour of topical debate with a virtual audience from around the UK. On the panel are Minister for Apprenticeships and Skills Gillian Keegan MP, Shadow Secretary of State for Housing Lucy Powell MP, Greek politician and economist Yanis Varoufakis, entrepreneur and founder of an IT company specialising in healthcare Kavita Oberoi and political commentator Frank Luntz

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Adam Fleming, Laura Kuenssberg and Chris Mason present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: Our Cops in the North
programme.desc: A fire that kills a man and could have led to more deaths appears to be arson and the culprit is likely to strike again - but identifying the criminal proves tricky as all the evidence was destroyed in the blaze. Meanwhile, a neighbourhood team gathers enough intelligence to raid the address of a local suspected drug dealer, and paramedics are prevented from carrying out CPR

programme.desc: Martel Maxwell is in her native Scotland looking round a flat in Dumfries, as well as visiting a plot of land in Birmingham, and Martin Roberts is at a house in Abergavenny

programme.desc: Luke and Carl visit a north London bar in search of money owed to a company who repaired their equipment but allegedly didn't pay for the work.
