programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: A plastic bag heat sealer that convicted an online drug trafficker. Plus, a casual chat with a criminal and a map join the dots to catch three burglars terrorising a neighbourhood

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion looks back at a selection of childhood antiques and collectables bought by past contestants. Anita Manning learns about the childhood game of marbles, while Natasha Raskin Sharp chats to expert Tim Weeks about collecting toys

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Luca tries to help a patient who is suffering in silence. Medical drama series set in a busy Midlands surgery, with Ross McLaren

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants Jason, Mark, Kiran and Angela vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson is in the Shropshire countryside with a couple of parents who have £575,000 to spend on a home for them and their young son. While in the county, Jules visits the Acton Scott Estate, home to an authentic Victorian working farm, and tries his hand at being a wheelwright's apprentice

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Rajeena and Hardeep have moved to their dream house in Great Glen boasting incredible views. However, its garden is an uninspiring blank canvas with an ugly bank of grass that obscures their vista rather than the traditional garden with a modern twist that they would prefer. The bank needs sorting and they want an entertaining area that capitalises on their view across the fields. A water feature and privacy is also important to the pair. Brothers David and Harry Rich compete with Charlie Dimmock to have their designs accepted to transform the patch on a £3,500 budget

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Izzie Balmer explore the antiques emporia of Lincolnshire and Nottinghamshire. Phil has his eye on a few quality antique staples, as well as one hefty horticultural item. Meanwhile, Izzie finds herself in what could well be her perfect shop, containing lots and lots of her favourite sparkly pieces. A magnificent Elizabethan Country House provides the backdrop for their final face-off

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Turkey v Italy (Kick-off 8.00pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of the opening game of the delayed tournament, a Group A fixture that takes place at Stadio Olimpico in Rome. Due to the worldwide coronavirus pandemic, the competition was postponed for a year, but is now finally set to get under way with this curtain-raiser in the Italian capital. Turkey will be looking for a better showing than 2016, when they finished third in a group also containing Croatia, Spain and Czech Republic, and failed to qualify for the last 16 as one of the best third-placed teams, losing out to Northern Ireland on goal difference. Italy, runners-up in 2012, are the group favourites this time around, with head coach and former Manchester City boss Roberto Mancini leading the Azzurri to a major tournament for the first time, following their failure to qualify for the 2018 World Cup in Russia. That marked the first time in 60 years that the four-time world champions had not appeared at FIFA's prestigious eve

programme.title: Elis James' Feast of Football
programme.desc: On the eve of Wales' opening game of Euro 2020 against Switzerland, Elis presents a live TV version of his cult podcast alongside regular guest Danny Gabbidon

programme.title: Crouchy's Year Late Euros: Live
programme.desc: With the delayed Euros finally on, former England striker Peter Crouch joins forces with Maya Jama and Alex Horne to offer their own unique take on the tournament with celebrity guests, players and highlights. Crouchy also spends some time with the Home Nation players before they head off to compete and expect some challenges and a few surprises along the way during this festival of football and fun

programme.title: Wild Weekends
programme.desc: BBC Radio 1 DJ Jeremiah Asiamah pushes three complete strangers outside of their comfort zones on a voyage of self-discovery in Torridon in the Highlands. Jeremiah's friend and outdoorsman Zeki Basan shows them how to set up camp under the stars, offers alternative cooking methods and helps them conquer an epic climb of more than 2,000ft

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: Turkey v Italy. Another chance to see the opening game of the delayed tournament, a Group A fixture that took place at Stadio Olimpico in Rome

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Ben Tish and Anna Haugh and special guest Emma Bunton. Helen McGinn joins via video link and picks the wines to go with the studio dishes

programme.title: Trooping the Colour
programme.desc: Live coverage of the pageantry and spectacle of the ceremony to mark the official birthday of Queen Elizabeth II, performed by British and Commonwealth army regiments

programme.title: Hairy Bikers Short
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers hop on their motorbikes for a culinary voyage of discovery along the Namibian coastline. They set up a traditional barbecue known as a braai in the Germanic town of Swakopmund, finding the best spot to cook among the dunes by taking to the air in a microlight

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Comedian Tudur Owen rewinds the funniest, most fascinating clips from half a century and more of Welsh television, here discovering the truth about football in Wales. It's a story of weekend warriors, boggy pitches and dodgy tackles, full of lively characters from the nation's TV past

programme.title: Wales: The Big Kick Off
programme.desc: Gabby Logan and Nathan Blake present this curtain-raiser to the Euro 2020 tournament, ahead of the national side's first game against Switzerland. They are joined in the studio by current and former Wales internationals to reminisce about the heroics of 2016 and get their take on this competition, and check in on the squad from their training camp in Cardiff. Plus, the Alarm perform the official Euro 2020 single, The Red Wall of Cymru

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Wales v Switzerland (Kick-off 2.00pm). Gabby Logan presents coverage of the opening Group A fixture for both sides, which takes place at Olympic Stadium in Baku, Azerbaijan. Wales are entering their second consecutive European Championship, having never previously qualified before their memorable debut appearance in 2016. They emerged top of a group also containing England, Slovakia and Russia, and defeated Northern Ireland in the last 16 before an unforgettable victory over heavy favourites Belgium in the quarter-finals. Chris Coleman's team eventually succumbed to Cristiano Ronaldo and Portugal in the semi-finals, but their remarkable run to the last four will live long in the memory for their fans. Switzerland have qualified for their fifth European Championship, and will be looking to make it to the knockout rounds for the second consecutive time. They pushed Poland hard in the last 16 in 2016, but eventually lost 5-4 on penalties. Arsenal's Granit Xhaka was the only player to miss

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Denmark v Finland (Kick-off 5.00pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of the opening game of Group B, which takes place at Parken, Copenhagen. The Danes famously won this trophy back in 1992, but have only reached the knockout rounds once since then. They failed to qualify for Euro 2016 in France, and finished third in their group at the 2012 tournament in Poland and Ukraine, despite beating the Netherlands in their opening match. Finland are making their debut at the European Championship, having finished second behind Italy in their qualifying group. Their talismanic striker Teemu Pukki will be hoping to make an impact on the biggest stage, having starred for Norwich City since his arrival in 2018. Micah Richards, Alex Scott and Cesc Fabregas provide analysis, with commentary from Jonathan Pearce and Martin Keown

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present a celebrity edition of the music-based quiz show, with comedian Susan Calman, former footballers John Barnes and Chris Kamara, and DJs Vick Hope, Melvin Odoom and Scott Mills. The pressure's on to name as many hit songs and artists as possible to win £10,000 for their chosen charities

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: A couple from Manchester answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Hosted by Danny Dyer

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as Dr Christian Jessen, Dr Ranj Singh, Matt Richardson, Andrew Maxwell, Katie Thistleton, Michelle Ackerley, Mark Moraghan and Tina Hobley take part in the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Ollie is brought into the ED once again with mysterious symptoms, and Rash becomes suspicious of Rosa's behaviour, leaving him convinced she is deliberately poisoning Ollie. Ethan once again pushes for Fenisha to spend time with Matthew instead of himself, and a construction worker brings her baby to the site, and leaves her in a shed that is about to be demolished

programme.title: Elis James' Feast of Football
programme.desc: Regular guest Danny Gabbidon joins the presents in Cardiff for reaction to Wales' opening match of Euro 2020 against Switzerland

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Highlights

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Bobby arrives at the restaurant for another date with Dana, only for Iqra to drop him in it by suggesting that he should cook them dinner. Rainie tells Vi to go back home - only to be caught off guard when the old busybody lets her in on a secret. programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Sara Davies are joined by former Dragon Theo Paphitis, who standing in for a self-isolating Peter Jones. The Dragons are served a selection of premium spiced rums by a confident drinks entrepreneur. A nursing bra designed to help mothers and babies breastfeed effectively and a heatless hair curling device invented in a garden shed are also offered up, while a Midlands-based duo pitch a voice-enhanced app for learner-readers. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Launch Show
programme.desc: Gary Lineker and guests look ahead to this summer's European Championship, which begins in Rome with the curtain-raiser between Turkey and Italy. The tournament, which was delayed by a year because of the coronavirus pandemic, will take place in 11 different cities across the continent, with the semi-finals and final staged at London's Wembley Stadium. England have only ever won one knockout tie at the Euros, but hopes are high Gareth Southgate's side can improve that record. The Three Lions, who boast a formidable array of attacking options, begin their campaign against Croatia on Sunday. Scotland meet the Czech Republic in their opener, while Wales face Switzerland. Gary is joined by former England defender Micah Richards, ex-Scotland star James McFadden and Ashley Williams, who captained Wales at Euro 2016

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents an hour of topical debate with a virtual audience from around the UK. On the panel are Minister for Apprenticeships and Skills Gillian Keegan MP, Shadow Secretary of State for Housing Lucy Powell MP, Greek politician and economist Yanis Varoufakis, entrepreneur and founder of an IT company specialising in healthcare Kavita Oberoi and political commentator Frank Luntz

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Adam Fleming, Laura Kuenssberg and Chris Mason present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: Our Cops in the North
programme.desc: A fire that kills a man and could have led to more deaths appears to be arson and the culprit is likely to strike again - but identifying the criminal proves tricky as all the evidence was destroyed in the blaze. Meanwhile, a neighbourhood team gathers enough intelligence to raid the address of a local suspected drug dealer, and paramedics are prevented from carrying out CPR

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell is in her native Scotland looking round a flat in Dumfries, as well as visiting a plot of land in Birmingham, and Martin Roberts is at a house in Abergavenny

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: Luke and Carl visit a north London bar in search of money owed to a company who repaired their equipment but allegedly didn't pay for the work. James and Mark meet a tricky debtor at a car lot who makes extraordinary demands, while Ben and Myles go in search of money believed to be owed by a cleaning company to a former employee

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: A plastic bag heat sealer that convicted an online drug trafficker. Plus, a casual chat with a criminal and a map join the dots to catch three burglars terrorising a neighbourhood

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion looks back at a selection of childhood antiques and collectables bought by past contestants. Anita Manning learns about the childhood game of marbles, while Natasha Raskin Sharp chats to expert Tim Weeks about collecting toys

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Luca tries to help a patient who is suffering in silence. Medical drama series set in a busy Midlands surgery, with Ross McLaren

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants Jason, Mark, Kiran and Angela vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson is in the Shropshire countryside with a couple of parents who have £575,000 to spend on a home for them and their young son. While in the county, Jules visits the Acton Scott Estate, home to an authentic Victorian working farm, and tries his hand at being a wheelwright's apprentice

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Rajeena and Hardeep have moved to their dream house in Great Glen boasting incredible views. However, its garden is an uninspiring blank canvas with an ugly bank of grass that obscures their vista rather than the traditional garden with a modern twist that they would prefer. The bank needs sorting and they want an entertaining area that capitalises on their view across the fields. A water feature and privacy is also important to the pair. Brothers David and Harry Rich compete with Charlie Dimmock to have their designs accepted to transform the patch on a £3,500 budget

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Izzie Balmer explore the antiques emporia of Lincolnshire and Nottinghamshire. Phil has his eye on a few quality antique staples, as well as one hefty horticultural item. Meanwhile, Izzie finds herself in what could well be her perfect shop, containing lots and lots of her favourite sparkly pieces. A magnificent Elizabethan Country House provides the backdrop for their final face-off

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Reporting Scotland; Weather
programme.desc: Presented by Sally Magnusson

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Turkey v Italy (Kick-off 8.00pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of the opening game of the delayed tournament, a Group A fixture that takes place at Stadio Olimpico in Rome. Due to the worldwide coronavirus pandemic, the competition was postponed for a year, but is now finally set to get under way with this curtain-raiser in the Italian capital. Turkey will be looking for a better showing than 2016, when they finished third in a group also containing Croatia, Spain and Czech Republic, and failed to qualify for the last 16 as one of the best third-placed teams, losing out to Northern Ireland on goal difference. Italy, runners-up in 2012, are the group favourites this time around, with head coach and former Manchester City boss Roberto Mancini leading the Azzurri to a major tournament for the first time, following their failure to qualify for the 2018 World Cup in Russia. That marked the first time in 60 years that the four-time world champions had not appeared at FIFA's prestigious eve

programme.title: Crouchy's Year Late Euros: Live
programme.desc: With the delayed Euros finally on, former England striker Peter Crouch joins forces with Maya Jama and Alex Horne to offer their own unique take on the tournament with celebrity guests, players and highlights. Crouchy also spends some time with the Home Nation players before they head off to compete and expect some challenges and a few surprises along the way during this festival of football and fun

programme.title: Scot Squad
programme.desc: As the Euros soccer tournament begins, the squad members deal with everything from rule violation to public jubilation. On the ground at the local station, Bobby seeks urgent police assistance from Officer Karen on tournament etiquette. At the top of high command, Head Coach Chief Miekelson delivers advice to the Scottish people on the optimum way to stay legal and to the Scotland side on the best way to win the tournament. Spoof documentary, starring Jack Docherty, Grado, Manjot Sumal, Jordan Young, Sally Reid, Karen Bartke, Darren Connell, Louise McCarthy and Julie Wilson Nimmo

programme.title: A View from the Euros
programme.desc: The View from the Terrace team offers an irreverent look at Scotland's fortunes in Euro 2020. Robert Borthwick unpacks his Euro Social Media Survival Guide and the boys try to make the football-fest even better in See Ya Later Monsieur Debater. Fake Passport has them pitching for viewers' new favourite second teams before they compete tournament-style for The Frenkie de Jong Euro Gong in Le Banker

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: Turkey v Italy. Another chance to see the opening game of the delayed tournament, a Group A fixture that took place at Stadio Olimpico in Rome

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Ben Tish and Anna Haugh and special guest Emma Bunton. Helen McGinn joins via video link and picks the wines to go with the studio dishes

programme.title: Trooping the Colour
programme.desc: Live coverage of the pageantry and spectacle of the ceremony to mark the official birthday of Queen Elizabeth II, performed by British and Commonwealth army regiments

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell, Martin Roberts and Dion Dublin examine a two-bedroom flat in Godalming, a house in a terrible state of repair in Telford, and a terraced property in Smethwick

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Ben Cooper and Gary Pe help contestants seek out items of value at the East of England Arena in Peterborough to sell for profit at auction. Plus, presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp shows off an interesting find with a family connection

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Wales v Switzerland (Kick-off 2.00pm). Gabby Logan presents coverage of the opening Group A fixture for both sides, which takes place at Olympic Stadium in Baku, Azerbaijan. Wales are entering their second consecutive European Championship, having never previously qualified before their memorable debut appearance in 2016. They emerged top of a group also containing England, Slovakia and Russia, and defeated Northern Ireland in the last 16 before an unforgettable victory over heavy favourites Belgium in the quarter-finals. Chris Coleman's team eventually succumbed to Cristiano Ronaldo and Portugal in the semi-finals, but their remarkable run to the last four will live long in the memory for their fans. Switzerland have qualified for their fifth European Championship, and will be looking to make it to the knockout rounds for the second consecutive time. They pushed Poland hard in the last 16 in 2016, but eventually lost 5-4 on penalties. Arsenal's Granit Xhaka was the only player to miss

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Denmark v Finland (Kick-off 5.00pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of the opening game of Group B, which takes place at Parken, Copenhagen. The Danes famously won this trophy back in 1992, but have only reached the knockout rounds once since then. They failed to qualify for Euro 2016 in France, and finished third in their group at the 2012 tournament in Poland and Ukraine, despite beating the Netherlands in their opening match. Finland are making their debut at the European Championship, having finished second behind Italy in their qualifying group. Their talismanic striker Teemu Pukki will be hoping to make an impact on the biggest stage, having starred for Norwich City since his arrival in 2018. Micah Richards, Alex Scott and Cesc Fabregas provide analysis, with commentary from Jonathan Pearce and Martin Keown

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present a celebrity edition of the music-based quiz show, with comedian Susan Calman, former footballers John Barnes and Chris Kamara, and DJs Vick Hope, Melvin Odoom and Scott Mills. The pressure's on to name as many hit songs and artists as possible to win £10,000 for their chosen charities

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: A couple from Manchester answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Hosted by Danny Dyer

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as Dr Christian Jessen, Dr Ranj Singh, Matt Richardson, Andrew Maxwell, Katie Thistleton, Michelle Ackerley, Mark Moraghan and Tina Hobley take part in the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Ollie is brought into the ED once again with mysterious symptoms, and Rash becomes suspicious of Rosa's behaviour, leaving him convinced she is deliberately poisoning Ollie. Ethan once again pushes for Fenisha to spend time with Matthew instead of himself, and a construction worker brings her baby to the site, and leaves her in a shed that is about to be demolished

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Highlights
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents highlights of the day's three games - Wales v Switzerland in Group A, and Denmark v Finland and Belgium v Russia in Group B. Wales won their most recent match against the Swiss in 2011, and were hoping to make a winning start to their campaign in Baku. The Russians will have been out for revenge against a star-studded Belgian side, who beat them twice in qualifying

programme.title: Thierry Henry: My France, My Euros
programme.desc: The former France striker and Arsenal's all-time leading scorer looks at European Championship stories, assessing the social and cultural impact football has across the continent. First shown as Henry's home country prepared to host Euro 2016 following November 2015's terror attacks in Paris, he explores how the beautiful game can unify a nation, from the success of the Netherlands in 1988 to Euro 96 in England, when 'football came home'. Contributors include Cesc Fabregas, Peter Schmeichel, Ruud Gullit, Jurgen Klinsmann, Alan Shearer, David Baddiel and Ian Broudie

programme.title: Alan Shearer's Euro 96: When Football Came Home
programme.desc: The former England striker looks back at the 1996 European Championship, in which he played a pivotal role in helping the host nation reach the semi-finals. programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Denmark v Finland (Kick-off 5.00pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of the opening game of Group B, which takes place at Parken, Copenhagen. The Danes famously won this trophy back in 1992, but have only reached the knockout rounds once since then. They failed to qualify for Euro 2016 in France, and finished third in their group at the 2012 tournament in Poland and Ukraine, despite beating the Netherlands in their opening match. Finland are making their debut at the European Championship, having finished second behind Italy in their qualifying group. Their talismanic striker Teemu Pukki will be hoping to make an impact on the biggest stage, having starred for Norwich City since his arrival in 2018. Micah Richards, Alex Scott and Cesc Fabregas provide analysis, with commentary from Jonathan Pearce and Martin Keown

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present a celebrity edition of the music-based quiz show, with comedian Susan Calman, former footballers John Barnes and Chris Kamara, and DJs Vick Hope, Melvin Odoom and Scott Mills. The pressure's on to name as many hit songs and artists as possible to win £10,000 for their chosen charities

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: A couple from Manchester answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Hosted by Danny Dyer

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as Dr Christian Jessen, Dr Ranj Singh, Matt Richardson, Andrew Maxwell, Katie Thistleton, Michelle Ackerley, Mark Moraghan and Tina Hobley take part in the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Ollie is brought into the ED once again with mysterious symptoms, and Rash becomes suspicious of Rosa's behaviour, leaving him convinced she is deliberately poisoning Ollie. Ethan once again pushes for Fenisha to spend time with Matthew instead of himself, and a construction worker brings her baby to the site, and leaves her in a shed that is about to be demolished

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Highlights
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents highlights of the day's three games - Wales v Switzerland in Group A, and Denmark v Finland and Belgium v Russia in Group B. Wales won their most recent match against the Swiss in 2011, and were hoping to make a winning start to their campaign in Baku. The Russians will have been out for revenge against a star-studded Belgian side, who beat them twice in qualifying

programme.title: Thierry Henry: My France, My Euros
programme.desc: The former France striker and Arsenal's all-time leading scorer looks at European Championship stories, assessing the social and cultural impact football has across the continent. First shown as Henry's home country prepared to host Euro 2016 following November 2015's terror attacks in Paris, he explores how the beautiful game can unify a nation, from the success of the Netherlands in 1988 to Euro 96 in England, when 'football came home'. Contributors include Cesc Fabregas, Peter Schmeichel, Ruud Gullit, Jurgen Klinsmann, Alan Shearer, David Baddiel and Ian Broudie

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: Wales v Switzerland. Another chance to see the opening Group A fixture for both sides, which took place at Olympic Stadium in Baku, Azerbaijan

programme.title: Gudrun - A' Bhana-phrionnsa Lochlannach
programme.desc: Gudrun is learning to tie knots when King Sigurd asks her to tether a young goat. However, disaster strikes when the goat escapes

programme.title: Treubh an Tuathanais
programme.desc: Cailean, Ailig, Goraidh and Muireall become detectives today as Cailean's new toy, blanket and sticks have gone missing

programme.title: Tree Fu Tom
programme.desc: Muru poses a seemingly impossible challenge that will force the Rangers to think hard

programme.title: Padraig Post: Seirbheis Deis agus Sonraichte
programme.desc: The long-serving mailman has a remote control to deliver to Peigi's shop as her new assistant, a robot, has gone out of control and the control is needed to contain him

programme.title: Na Moomins/Moomin Valley
programme.desc: Mùmantrobha tries to play a trick on Beadag, but things go awry and his plan backfires

programme.title: A' Chuil
programme.desc: Calum from CBBC pays Choirstaidh a visit to find out more about the programme A' Chùil

programme.title: A' Chuil
programme.desc: Choirstaidh presents from her studio A' Chùil to show a great way of changing up clothes

programme.title: An Rud As Fheàrr Leam
programme.desc: Lilly from Lochaber talks about her favourite hobby, skiing

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news and information from BBC ALBA

programme.title: Farpaisean Chon-Chaorach (Sheepdog Trials)
programme.desc: Donald MacSween and Catriona MacPhee are in Stranraer for the second day of competition at the Scottish National sheepdog trial

programme.title: Slighean Sgoile an t-Saoghail (Most Dangerous Ways to School)
programme.desc: Lake Titicaca, straddling the border between Peru and Bolivia in the Andes Mountains, is one of South America's largest lakes. Living here are the Uro - an ancient people, described at times as the only decedents of the Incas. They are the people of the floating islands, a very small community of about 2,000 with a community consisting of 50 swimming islands. Every day from the furthest corners, the youngest inhabitants paddle their way in their small boats to school

programme.title: Celtic Connections
programme.desc: A Canadian trio perform one of their tunes mixing traditional-folk with a trance-folk beat

programme.title: Tir is Teanga (Language and Landscape)
programme.desc: Hillwalker Colin MacLeod travels to Perthshire, where he climbs Ben Lawers, which is situated between Loch Tay and Glen Lyon. He also explores the history behind the name given to the Schiehallion mountain, the place where 18th-century astronomer Nevil Maskelyne first worked out Earth's density

programme.title: Sgoil nan Sagart/Priest School
programme.desc: Following Ronald Campbell from Benbecula, who is studying for the priesthood in Rome

programme.title: Seirm
programme.desc: Freaturing acclaimed singer Robert Robertson, Irish supergroup Altan and vocalist Siobhan Wilson. Plus, Ruth Keggan from the Isle of Man and Flavia Coelho from Brazil. Hosted by Cathy MacDonald

programme.title: Ar Dobhair Fhein
programme.desc: Documentary looking at the events of the 1937 bothy fire in Kirkintilloch, which claimed the lives of 10 Irish potato diggers

programme.title: Dealbh is Slighe
programme.desc: Calum is in the heart of the Highlands in Glen Affric looking for a photo of one of Scotland's most ancient and mysterious trees - the elm

programme.title: Ceòl Dùthchasach/Ken Burns' Country Music
programme.desc: Biographies of the trailblazers who created and shaped Country music, from the Carter Family and Bob Wills to Hank Williams, Patsy Cline and Garth Brooks

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: Daily magazine, featuring a mix of celebrity chat, showbusiness news, lifestyle features, topical discussion, health and beauty advice and more

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: The women put the world to rights once more, discussing everything from politics to parenting, and invite a celebrity guest to chat about what they are up to - or anything else that takes their fancy

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and the dealers are in Llandudno, where James Layte battles to get his hands on an Omega watch and Mark Stevens puts his money down on a Clarice Cliff vase

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Warwick Davis in which a team of five friends from Brighton answers questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then tries to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Karen, David, Rosie and Joe pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser. Answering general knowledge questions, they each add as much money as they dare to the jackpot - but anyone caught by the Chaser is out for good, leaving fewer players to team up and play in the final chase

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Andrea's dog is in danger. Juliette has a proposal for Jimmy. Ben worries that Aaron's constant concern for Liv is taking its toll

programme.title: Tonight
programme.desc: Six months after the UK left Europe, where do we stand today? From immigration and the NHS, to jobs, imports and exports - what's happened to those big issues that drove the momentous vote? While we recover from the shock of the pandemic, economics editor Joel Hills examines the state of Global Britain looks, and reveals how close we are to securing lucrative trade deals around the world

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: The camping trip is off. Liam urges Tracy to look after herself, and it remains to be seen whether Nicola will fight for her marriage

programme.title: The Greek Islands with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: Julia visits the island of Crete, intending to step away from the tourist trail and head deep into its heart to explore the Dikti mountains and its striking plateau of windmills. She meets goat shepherd Giannis, who shows her how he makes cheese using traditional methods. Back on the coast at Rethymno, Julia tries the delicacy baklava, meeting filo pastry expert Giorgos, and also meets master artisan Nico, who expertly makes stringed musical instrument the Cretan lyra. Her journey ends on the stunning but poignant neighbouring island of Spinalonga - once an exiled leper colony

programme.title: Billion Pound Bond Street
programme.desc: Bond Street has been London's most exclusive shopping street for 300 years. The glamorous half mile between Oxford Street and Piccadilly boasts more luxury brands and more royal warrants than any other in Britain. In this documentary narrated by Jenna Coleman, cameras take a look at how this lucrative region has survived during the pandemic

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: The Late Debate
programme.desc: Simon Harris is joined by a panel of local MPs for a discussion on the latest news and events at Westminster

programme.title: England's Grand
programme.desc: Gareth Southgate, Harry Kane and Ian Wright join a host of players, authors and experts for an A-Z journey through the history of the England football team. The documentary brings together previously unheard stories, anecdotes and unearthed archive in a celebration of the 147 years leading up to England's 1,000th match, which took place on Thursday 14th November 2019 against Montenegro

programme.title: ITV Football Classics
programme.desc: Gabriel Clarke introduces action from the semi-final at Wembley Stadium, which was decided by a penalty shootout. Bob Wilson presented the coverage, with analysis from Glenn Hoddle, Jack Charlton and John Barnes, and commentary by Brian Moore and Kevin Keegan

programme.desc: Short bulletin rounding up the day's showbusiness stories

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Five university students from Coventry answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Hosted by Warwick Davis

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Meena opens up to Diane, there is a glimpse into Ms Jutla's past, and Vinny gets a call he was waiting for

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Tyrone confronts Fiz, accusing her of trying to have him arrested in a bid to bolster her chances of custody. A grief-stricken Nina lashes out at Summer and hits the bottle. Daisy attempts to see off potential buyers at the Rovers

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack investigates the abduction of a bus driver and his conductor and an unrelated murder of a children's entertainer, but is frustrated to find himself working alongside a former colleague who got him suspended by making a complaint about his handling of a case. Long-running crime drama, with David Jason, and co-starring Cherie Lunghi and John Lyons

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Highlights
programme.desc: Turkey v Italy. Mark Pougatch presents action from the opening game of the tournament, a Group A fixture that took place at Stadio Olimpico in Rome. There is also a comprehensive preview of the competition, focusing on England, Scotland and Wales - all together in a major tournament for the first time since 1958. Eni Aluko, Scott Brown and Robert Earnshaw provide analysis, with commentary from Seb Hutchinson and Emma Hayes

programme.title: FYI Extra
programme.desc: Short bulletin rounding up the day's showbusiness stories

programme.title: Ben 10
programme.desc: Team Tennyson trade campfire stories in an anthology-style series that pits Ben against his arch-rival Kevin 11.

programme.desc: Four ninja warriors battle dark Lord Garmadon and his skeleton army

programme.title: NinjaGo: Masters of Spinjitzu
programme.desc: Four ninja warriors battle dark Lord Garmadon and his skeleton army

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Weekend Best!
programme.desc: Singer-songwriter Tom Odell joins hosts Martin and Roman Kemp on the entertainment show, while resident chef Shivi Ramoutar helps the father and son develop their culinary skills

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James is joined in the kitchen by singers Roachford and Beverley Knight, and chefs Lenny Carr-Roberts and Lesley Waters. James prepares red mullet, mussels and salmon, presides over a masterclass in batter, and takes a culinary trip to Edinburgh

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner have recipes for summertime centrepieces for entertaining guests, including slow-cooked beef rendang, courgette and pea fritters, prawns served in a bread bowl, a ginger mocktail, and ricotta doughnuts with chocolate sauce

programme.title: James Martin's French Adventure
programme.desc: The chef visits Paris, where he explores one of the largest wholesale food markets in Europe, before cooking a dish using chicken and mushrooms. James also visits the kitchen of the world-renowned chef, Pierre Gagnaire, and cooks a duck breast, complemented by tangy vegetables and chilli sauce

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin presents coverage of racing from York, including the 3.05 Grand Cup. Plus, meeting from Chester and Sandown Park. With analysis from Jason Weaver and Adele Mulrennan, reports by Natalie Green and Oli Bell, and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Jerome, Scott, Alexia and Mandy pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser. Answering general knowledge questions, they each add as much money as they dare to the jackpot - but anyone caught by the Chaser is out for good, leaving fewer players to team up and play in the final chase

programme.title: In for a Penny
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern heads to Somerset or the game show in which he travels around the UK setting members of the public unusual challenges, giving them the chance to win anything from the price of their petrol to £1,000 in the final game. There are previously unseen games from three of the cities he has visited in this series

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Live
programme.desc: Belgium v Russia (Kick-off 8.00pm). Coverage of both sides' opening Group B match, which takes place at Saint Petersburg Stadium. Roberto Martinez's Belgium enter this tournament as one of the favourites for glory, with star names including Eden Hazard, Kevin De Bruyne, Romelu Lukaku and Thibaut Courtois among their ranks. However, their squad is statistically the oldest at the tournament, and this could be the final opportunity for their so-called 'golden generation' to claim a major trophy. Russia reached the semi-finals back in 2008, but have not made it past the group stage since then, and finished bottom of a pool also containing Wales, England and Slovakia at Euro 2016 in France. The 2018 World Cup hosts lost only two games in qualifying, incidentally both to Belgium, so they will be looking for revenge here. Mark Pougatch is joined by Roy Keane, Ian Wright and Patrick Vieira, with commentary from Sam Matterface and Lee Dixon

programme.title: Notting Hill (1999)
programme.desc: A recently divorced bookshop owner finds love with a glamorous Hollywood film star, but ends up the focus of unwanted media interest - which makes maintaining a relationship a tricky proposition. Romantic comedy written by Richard Curtis, starring Hugh Grant, Julia Roberts, Rhys Ifans, Emma Chambers, James Dreyfus and Gina McKee

programme.title: Britain's Brightest Family
programme.desc: Having made it through to the quarter-final, two bright families go head to head for a place in the semis of the knockout tournament. Hosted by Anne 'The Governess' Hegerty

programme.title: Ben 10
programme.desc: Team Tennyson trade campfire stories in an anthology-style series that pits Ben against his arch-rival Kevin 11.

programme.title: NinjaGo: Masters of Spinjitzu
programme.desc: Four ninja warriors battle dark Lord Garmadon and his skeleton army

programme.title: NinjaGo: Masters of Spinjitzu
programme.desc: Four ninja warriors battle dark Lord Garmadon and his skeleton army

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Weekend Best!
programme.desc: Singer-songwriter Tom Odell joins hosts Martin and Roman Kemp on the entertainment show, while resident chef Shivi Ramoutar helps the father and son develop their culinary skills

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James is joined in the kitchen by singers Roachford and Beverley Knight, and chefs Lenny Carr-Roberts and Lesley Waters. James prepares red mullet, mussels and salmon, presides over a masterclass in batter, and takes a culinary trip to Edinburgh

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner have recipes for summertime centrepieces for entertaining guests, including slow-cooked beef rendang, courgette and pea fritters, prawns served in a bread bowl, a ginger mocktail, and ricotta doughnuts with chocolate sauce

programme.title: James Martin's French Adventure
programme.desc: The chef visits Paris, where he explores one of the largest wholesale food markets in Europe, before cooking a dish using chicken and mushrooms. James also visits the kitchen of the world-renowned chef, Pierre Gagnaire, and cooks a duck breast, complemented by tangy vegetables and chilli sauce

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin presents coverage of racing from York, including the 3.05 Grand Cup. Plus, meeting from Chester and Sandown Park. With analysis from Jason Weaver and Adele Mulrennan, reports by Natalie Green and Oli Bell, and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Jerome, Scott, Alexia and Mandy pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser. Answering general knowledge questions, they each add as much money as they dare to the jackpot - but anyone caught by the Chaser is out for good, leaving fewer players to team up and play in the final chase

programme.title: In for a Penny
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern heads to Somerset or the game show in which he travels around the UK setting members of the public unusual challenges, giving them the chance to win anything from the price of their petrol to £1,000 in the final game. There are previously unseen games from three of the cities he has visited in this series

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Live
programme.desc: Belgium v Russia (Kick-off 8.00pm). Coverage of both sides' opening Group B match, which takes place at Saint Petersburg Stadium. Roberto Martinez's Belgium enter this tournament as one of the favourites for glory, with star names including Eden Hazard, Kevin De Bruyne, Romelu Lukaku and Thibaut Courtois among their ranks. However, their squad is statistically the oldest at the tournament, and this could be the final opportunity for their so-called 'golden generation' to claim a major trophy. Russia reached the semi-finals back in 2008, but have not made it past the group stage since then, and finished bottom of a pool also containing Wales, England and Slovakia at Euro 2016 in France. The 2018 World Cup hosts lost only two games in qualifying, incidentally both to Belgium, so they will be looking for revenge here. Mark Pougatch is joined by Roy Keane, Ian Wright and Patrick Vieira, with commentary from Sam Matterface and Lee Dixon

programme.title: Notting Hill (1999)
programme.desc: A recently divorced bookshop owner finds love with a glamorous Hollywood film star, but ends up the focus of unwanted media interest - which makes maintaining a relationship a tricky proposition. Romantic comedy written by Richard Curtis, starring Hugh Grant, Julia Roberts, Rhys Ifans, Emma Chambers, James Dreyfus and Gina McKee

programme.title: Tomb Raider (2018)
programme.desc: After her father's disappearance, kickboxing heiress Lara Croft makes money as a bike courier and refuses to claim her inheritance, convinced her dad is still alive. But when she learns the country estate could be sold off unless she claims it, she reluctantly agrees - and finds a pre-recorded message from her father giving clues to his possible whereabouts. Determined to find him, she sets out across the sea to Hong Kong - but soon crosses paths with a small army of mercenaries determined to break into a supposedly haunted tomb. Action adventure based on the video game, starring Alicia Vikander, Dominic West, Walton Goggins and Kristin Scott Thomas

programme.title: Tomb Raider (2018)
programme.desc: After her father's disappearance, kickboxing heiress Lara Croft makes money as a bike courier and refuses to claim her inheritance, convinced her dad is still alive. But when she learns the country estate could be sold off unless she claims it, she reluctantly agrees - and finds a pre-recorded message from her father giving clues to his possible whereabouts. Determined to find him, she sets out across the sea to Hong Kong - but soon crosses paths with a small army of mercenaries determined to break into a supposedly haunted tomb. Action adventure based on the video game, starring Alicia Vikander, Dominic West, Walton Goggins and Kristin Scott Thomas

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A philandering husband is found dying by the swimming pool after being shot during a house party at the home of Lady Lucy Angkatell. Legendary Belgian Sleuth Poirot, who is guest of honour at the gathering, appears to have an open-and-shut case on his hands - but the investigator finds himself up against one of his finest adversaries. Whodunit, guest-starring Sarah Miles (Ryan's Daughter), with David Suchet, Lysette Anthony and Edward Fox

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective finds himself investigating the strange and gruesome murder of an elderly psychiatrist. Elsewhere, Poirot's old friend Ariadne Oliver is being pressed to uncover the truth behind two decade-old deaths. As the pair dig deep for evidence, they soon discover that their separate investigations are linked and join forces to solve the overarching mystery. David Suchet, Zoe Wanamaker and Greta Scacchi star

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective is drawn into Newcastle's underworld to discover the identity of a murder victim found dead in the river, with no clue as to who he is apart from a distinctive drugs wrapper. She discovers the dead man had ties to some very dangerous people, many of whom may have wanted him dead, while a witness's testimony links the case to a remote farm where a murder took place 13 years previously. The connection brings Kenny's past back to haunt him, as he was responsible for seeing the perpetrator convicted

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: When Grace Phillips is killed in an explosion in a munitions factory, Foyle is asked by one of her co-workers to investigate the death. Initially he concludes a tragic accident has occurred until another woman is found murdered and Foyle starts to believe the deaths are connected. Wartime drama, starring Michael Kitchen

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth investigates the murder of her old friend Father Gorman, using a list of names sent by the priest moments before his death to bring the guilty party to justice. Following a trail of clues to London, Marple is drawn to the Pale Horse Inn - where she discovers a sinister world of occult practices. Drama, starring Julia McKenzie in the title role, with Lynda Baron, Pauline Collins, Nicholas Parsons and Neil Pearson

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is reunited with sidekick Captain Hastings (Hugh Fraser), secretary Miss Lemon (Pauline Moran) and Inspector Japp (Philip Jackson) in a case that plunges him into the world of global espionage as the Second World War looms. The public are in panic after the shocking death of Russian grandmaster Ivan Savaranoff during a game of chess. Poirot must try to determine the good guys from the bad in a complex plot that sees the main figures used like pawns. David Suchet stars in a story adapted by Mark Gatiss and Ian Hallard

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is reunited with old friend Ariadne Oliver, who has been commissioned to stage a game of murder hunt at a summer fete in the grounds of Nasse House, the recently purchased home of wealthy financier Sir George Stubbs. However, the novelist's instincts tell her she's getting involved in something much darker - and she's proved right when the girl volunteering to play the part of the victim really does turn out to be dead. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Zoe Wanamaker, Sean Pertwee, Martin Jarvis, Sam Kelly, Sinead Cusack, Rosalind Ayres and Tom Ellis

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Obsessive-compulsive Mrs Tishell returns to the village, but her affection for Martin quickly disappears when he refuses to pass her fit for work. As if he didn't have enough on his plate, the grumpy doctor must also face his fear of blood when tests show that Lorna has a high level of ferritin, which is placing a strain on her heart and liver. Meanwhile, PC Penhale hopes to be selected for an elite police squad by taking part in a survival training course - but ends up shooting himself in the foot

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Hypochondriac Malcolm Rayner is struggling with his breathing and Martin asks him if he has ever worked with asbestos. Penhale then causes pandemonium by putting up warning notices about the substance's harmful properties throughout the village. Meanwhile, Bert pops the question to Jennifer, and Ruth finds fault with Al's business proposal. Guest starring Christopher Fairbank (Auf Wiedersehen, Pet)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: The detective's god-daughter Lydia and her son James - who is severely traumatised by a bombing at his school - come to stay unexpectedly. Struggling to look after the lad when his mother goes missing, Foyle's life suffers a further complication when the body of a local man is found near a military centre - leading him into the top-secret world of weapons research. Kate Fleetwood and Michael Jayston guest star, with Michael Kitchen and Honeysuckle Weeks

programme.title: Carry On Cowboy (1966)
programme.desc: Notorious outlaw the Rumpo Kid runs riot in lawless Stodge City. A local judge sends for someone to clean up the town - a summons taken literally by a timid English sanitation expert who becomes the new sheriff. programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Hollywood actress Marina Gregg and her director husband take up residence at Gossington Hall in the village of St Mary Mead and hold a reception to meet their new neighbours, including the hall's previous owner Dolly Bantry. The celebrations turn to tragedy, however, after a fan fatally drinks a poisoned cocktail apparently meant for the new lady of the manor. programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: After enjoying a trip to the theatre with Captain Hastings, the detective is disturbed by the sound of gunshots in an apartment at Whitehaven Mansions, and soon finds himself investigating a murder. Drama, starring David Suchet, Hugh Fraser and Philip Jackson

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A young woman asks Poirot to investigate her father's murder - a 14-year-old crime for which her mother was found guilty and executed. The detective agrees to investigate, but warns his client that he may discover things she does not want to hear. David Suchet stars, with Rachael Stirling, Aidan Gillen, Aimee Mullens, Toby Stephens, Gemma Jones, Patrick Malahide and Annette Badland

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective investigates a double poisoning, aiming to prove the innocence of Elinor Carlisle, the chief suspect in the deaths of her aunt and her rival in love. However, it emerges that she is the only person with a motive, opportunity and means to administer the poison. Crime drama, starring David Suchet, Elisabeth Dermot-Walsh, Diana Quick, Paul McGann and Rupert Penry-Jones

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The dapper Belgian sleuth goes on an Egyptian cruise on board an old steamer, but when an American heiress is murdered, his little grey cells go into overdrive. On questioning each of his fellow passengers, he realises many of them have secrets they want to remain hidden. David Suchet stars, with James Fox, Judy Parfitt, Emily Blunt, Daisy Donovan, Frances de la Tour and David Soul

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The owner of local country magazine Midsomer Life becomes a suspect when a man's body is discovered in woodland, and it is established the victim had been having an affair with his wife. As Barnaby and Jones delve into the case, another murder forces them to reassess their inquiries. Detective drama, starring John Nettles, Jason Hughes and Simon Williams

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A magician's assistant drops dead on stage, poisoned by the toxins from a tropical frog. While Barnaby and Jones begin their investigations, tempers flare in the local community between Ernest, a firm believer in a mystical cult, and his former friend Aloysius - whose background in the jungles of South America makes him the prime suspect for the murder. Starring John Nettles, Ronald Pickup and Stuart Wilson

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Julia McKenzie returns as the sleuthing spinster in the first of three murder mysteries. While staying at a hotel in the Caribbean, Miss Marple investigates the death of a fellow guest, refusing to believe he died of a heart attack. With the help of a business tycoon she sets about finding the real cause of the man's demise, and is soon drawn into a web of deceit and dark magic. Adapted by Charlie Higson and co-starring Antony Sher, Robert Webb, Montserrat Lombard, Daniel Rigby, Hermione Norris, MyAnna Buring, Warren Brown and Oliver Ford Davies

programme.title: Sanditon
programme.desc: Andrew Davies's adaptation of Jane Austen's unfinished final novel about a spirited young woman who finds herself in a seaside resort on the cusp of dramatic change - and becomes involved in the scheming of its ambitious inhabitants. Charlotte and Sidney find themselves at loggerheads but must work together in an attempt to rescue Miss Lambe. Tom tries to save Sanditon and his marriage, while Edward and Clara enter into a scandalous pact to finally get their hands on Lady Denham's inheritance

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sally takes on overtime as an excuse to be near Greg. Alec offers to look after Rita so she can be discharged. Maxine hopes Greg will open up more during a romantic weekend

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sally is upset to discover Greg is going away with Maxine. Rita returns home and is frightened of being alone. Toyah asks Ken to give her private tutored lessons

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zoe needs evidence of Sean's wrongdoing. Kathy talks to Graham about giving up Alice. Chris asks his captor to set him free and his captor reveals their identity

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Alan discovers the antiques are missing and immediately suspects Eric. Paddy reveals his honeymoon plan to Marlon. Liam threatens to kill Chris

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Blaketon is threatened with demotion and ponders a seemingly perfect job with a security firm, but Nick is suspicious of the lucrative offer. Rural police drama, starring Nick Berry, Derek Fowlds and Bill Maynard

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Ida is pleased when a potential romantic suitor, a travelling sales rep, makes advances towards Miss Parfitt

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: The girls find it tough sharing with one man, but their problems double once Robin's friend Larry moves in. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: A local woman is killed when an experiment in biological warfare goes tragically wrong. Sam also falls victim to a potentially fatal illness as a result of the project. Foyle is forced to head into the most secretive and dangerous areas of wartime research, unearthing secrets that cannot be revealed even to the prime minister. Michael Kitchen stars, with Honeysuckle Weeks and Philip Franks

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A man admits to killing his wife after her body is found by the roadside, but Nick uncovers inconsistencies in the story. Rural police drama, starring Nick Berry, Derek Fowlds and Bill Maynard

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Spalding tells Angie to keep her nose out of Sean's case. Paddy invites Zoe to his stag night. Mandy confronts Eric about the stolen antiques when she finds the figurine

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Mandy and Paddy's wedding day arrives. Chris rages at Liam for going near Zoe. The wedding ceremony is interrupted when Eric arrives with the police

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Kevin complains that Sally is doing too much overtime. Spider and Audrey do battle on election day. Fred thinks he has found a loophole which will hand victory to Mrs Roberts

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Rita is upset to discover she is forgetting things. Audrey loves all the attention she is getting as a Councillor. Spider is stunned when Lorraine tells him she fancies him

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A philandering husband is found dying by the swimming pool after being shot during a house party at the home of Lady Lucy Angkatell. Legendary Belgian Sleuth Poirot, who is guest of honour at the gathering, appears to have an open-and-shut case on his hands - but the investigator finds himself up against one of his finest adversaries. Whodunit, guest-starring Sarah Miles (Ryan's Daughter), with David Suchet, Lysette Anthony and Edward Fox

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective finds himself investigating the strange and gruesome murder of an elderly psychiatrist. Elsewhere, Poirot's old friend Ariadne Oliver is being pressed to uncover the truth behind two decade-old deaths. As the pair dig deep for evidence, they soon discover that their separate investigations are linked and join forces to solve the overarching mystery. David Suchet, Zoe Wanamaker and Greta Scacchi star

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective is drawn into Newcastle's underworld to discover the identity of a murder victim found dead in the river, with no clue as to who he is apart from a distinctive drugs wrapper. She discovers the dead man had ties to some very dangerous people, many of whom may have wanted him dead, while a witness's testimony links the case to a remote farm where a murder took place 13 years previously. The connection brings Kenny's past back to haunt him, as he was responsible for seeing the perpetrator convicted

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: When Grace Phillips is killed in an explosion in a munitions factory, Foyle is asked by one of her co-workers to investigate the death. Initially he concludes a tragic accident has occurred until another woman is found murdered and Foyle starts to believe the deaths are connected. Wartime drama, starring Michael Kitchen

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth investigates the murder of her old friend Father Gorman, using a list of names sent by the priest moments before his death to bring the guilty party to justice. Following a trail of clues to London, Marple is drawn to the Pale Horse Inn - where she discovers a sinister world of occult practices. Drama, starring Julia McKenzie in the title role, with Lynda Baron, Pauline Collins, Nicholas Parsons and Neil Pearson

programme.desc: When Grace Phillips is killed in an explosion in a munitions factory, Foyle is asked by one of her co-workers to investigate the death. programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick discovers an act of courage can have sinister consequences after he rescues a woman from a house fire. Meanwhile, Greengrass sets himself up as a birdwatcher's retreat. Rural police drama, starring Nick Berry, Bill Maynard and Derek Fowlds

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Angie makes Eric drop the charges against Mandy. Chris tries to figure out who ordered Liam to kidnap him. The wedding reception descends into chaos

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zoe is a nervous wreck as she prepares to confront her brother's kidnapper, while Paddy's secret honeymoon plans are disrupted when the police demand Mandy's passport

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maxine and Greg return from their romantic weekend away. Kevin takes Nick on at the garage. Mike is shocked by Hayley's skills as a machinist and offers her a job

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Hayley is nervous when she starts work at Underworld. Alec is upset that Rita refuses to talk to him. Greg runs back into Maxine's arms when Sally cannot meet up with him

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is reunited with sidekick Captain Hastings (Hugh Fraser), secretary Miss Lemon (Pauline Moran) and Inspector Japp (Philip Jackson) in a case that plunges him into the world of global espionage as the Second World War looms. The public are in panic after the shocking death of Russian grandmaster Ivan Savaranoff during a game of chess. Poirot must try to determine the good guys from the bad in a complex plot that sees the main figures used like pawns. David Suchet stars in a story adapted by Mark Gatiss and Ian Hallard

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is reunited with old friend Ariadne Oliver, who has been commissioned to stage a game of murder hunt at a summer fete in the grounds of Nasse House, the recently purchased home of wealthy financier Sir George Stubbs. However, the novelist's instincts tell her she's getting involved in something much darker - and she's proved right when the girl volunteering to play the part of the victim really does turn out to be dead. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Zoe Wanamaker, Sean Pertwee, Martin Jarvis, Sam Kelly, Sinead Cusack, Rosalind Ayres and Tom Ellis

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Obsessive-compulsive Mrs Tishell returns to the village, but her affection for Martin quickly disappears when he refuses to pass her fit for work. As if he didn't have enough on his plate, the grumpy doctor must also face his fear of blood when tests show that Lorna has a high level of ferritin, which is placing a strain on her heart and liver. Meanwhile, PC Penhale hopes to be selected for an elite police squad by taking part in a survival training course - but ends up shooting himself in the foot

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Hypochondriac Malcolm Rayner is struggling with his breathing and Martin asks him if he has ever worked with asbestos. Penhale then causes pandemonium by putting up warning notices about the substance's harmful properties throughout the village. Meanwhile, Bert pops the question to Jennifer, and Ruth finds fault with Al's business proposal. Guest starring Christopher Fairbank (Auf Wiedersehen, Pet)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: The detective's god-daughter Lydia and her son James - who is severely traumatised by a bombing at his school - come to stay unexpectedly. Struggling to look after the lad when his mother goes missing, Foyle's life suffers a further complication when the body of a local man is found near a military centre - leading him into the top-secret world of weapons research. Kate Fleetwood and Michael Jayston guest star, with Michael Kitchen and Honeysuckle Weeks

programme.title: Carry On Cowboy (1966)
programme.desc: Notorious outlaw the Rumpo Kid runs riot in lawless Stodge City. A local judge sends for someone to clean up the town - a summons taken literally by a timid English sanitation expert who becomes the new sheriff. Comedy Western, starring Jim Dale, Sid James, Charles Hawtrey, Kenneth Williams, Bernard Bresslaw and Joan Sims

programme.title: World of Sport
programme.desc: Richard Henwood takes a look back at The Indoor League. A show featuring 1970s pub games, presented by Yorkshire cricketing great Fred Trueman.

programme.title: ITV Football Classics
programme.desc: Gabriel Clarke introduces extended highlights of the quarter-final match from Stade Pierre-Mauroy in Lille, France. Chris Coleman's side had impressed many to reach the knockout rounds, with Gareth Bale and Aaron Ramsey in starring roles, but it was their less-celebrated teammates who grabbed the headlines against the highly-fancied Belgians in this entertaining match. Commentary by Clive Tyldesley and Craig Bellamy

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: CI5 investigates a consortium building a lethal fighter plane in eastern Europe, that appears willing to kill to supress publication of the aircraft's appalling safetly record. Lynda Bellingham guest stars, with Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: The lure of easy money beckons once again as Terry and Arthur appreciate the commercial possibilities of cheap tobacco, but it is not long before the would-be smugglers are all at sea. Comedy, starring George Cole, Dennis Waterman and Ray Winstone

programme.title: The Champions
programme.desc: The team comes to the aid of scientists duped by crooks into creating a nuclear fission gun. Fantasy drama, starring William Gaunt, Alexandra Bastedo and Stuart Damon

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Emma is enticed into the imposing mansion of an elderly bridge expert, where she is forced to get by without Steed, who is at home nursing a sprained ankle. Ronald Lacey guest stars

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle are trained to act as bodyguards to a Middle Eastern president who is preparing to sign an important peace treaty. Meanwhile, two killers are receiving similar training to prepare them to assassinate him. As their paths cross, the CI5 operatives learn the would-be killers have some useful inside information and share more in common with them than they may have imagined

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur is repaid a debt in dodgy £20 notes, but by the time it comes to light they are counterfeit his creditor is heading on a flight to India. Once the Winchester is pinpointed as the source of the forged currency, Arthur also finds he has Chisholm breathing down his neck. Robbie Coltrane and Art Malik guest star, with George Cole and Patrick Malahide

programme.title: French Open Tennis: Roland-Garros Live
programme.desc: Coverage of the 12th day's play at Roland-Garros, featuring the women's singles semi-finals in the Grand Slam tournament. Both matches were decided in straight sets last year, with Iga Swiatek and Sofia Kenin defeating Nadia Podoroska and Petra Kvitova respectively. John Inverdale presents, with analysis from Jim Courier, Fabrice Santoro, Marion Bartoli, Mark Petchey, Anne Keothavong and Sam Smith, commentary by Nick Mullins and Jonathan Overend, and reports from Celina Hinchcliffe

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as one-time Coronation Street actor Jimi Mistry (Khalid Nazir), former Emmerdale star Gemma Atkinson (Carly Hope), Loose Women's Nadia Sawalha and veteran actor James Cosmo pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser, in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds for the charity of their choice. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: World Rally Championship Highlights
programme.desc: The fifth round of the season saw the action head to Italy, where rugged and sun-baked gravel tracks in the Mediterranean island of Sardinia ensured one of the season's sternest challenges. Fast and narrow stages left no margin for error, while intense heat combined with abrasive roads to test tyres to the limit.

programme.title: Die Hard 2 (1990)
programme.desc: Tough cop John McClane arrives at an airport to pick up his wife, but finds himself plunged into a bloody life-or-death struggle. He must battle terrorists who have seized control of the terminal as part of a campaign to secure the release of a convicted drug baron. Action thriller sequel, starring Bruce Willis, Bonnie Bedelia, William Sadler, Franco Nero and William Atherton

programme.title: FYI Daily
programme.desc: The latest news from the world of entertainment.

programme.title: The Avengers

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle's country holiday with their latest girlfriends spirals into chaos when they are held hostage in a farmhouse by two gunmen who have just botched a robbery. Adventure, with Gordon Jackson, Lewis Collins and Martin Shaw

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Apparently successful entrepreneur Clive Cosgrove loses his 'confidence' after Arthur and Terry attend his wedding celebrations. Vintage comedy drama, guest starring Patrick Mower, with George Cole and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: French Open Tennis: Roland-Garros Live
programme.desc: Coverage of the 13th day's play at Roland-Garros, featuring the men's singles semi-finals in the Grand Slam tournament. Not for the first time, Rafael Nadal reached last year's final without losing a set throughout the tournament, overcoming Diego Schwartzman 6-3, 6-3, 7-6 in the last four. It was Novak Djokovic who joined him in the showpiece match, but it took him five sets to eventually defeat Stefanos Tsitsipas. John Inverdale presents, with analysis from Jim Courier, Fabrice Santoro, Marion Bartoli, Mark Petchey, Anne Keothavong and Sam Smith, commentary by Nick Mullins and Jonathan Overend, and reports from Celina Hinchcliffe

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Jeremy Wade has hunted down freshwater killers for 30 years, but only now has he discovered he was sharing those waters with darker, deadlier creatures - parasites.

programme.title: The Italian Job (2003)
programme.desc: A crook double-crosses his partners in crime, a betrayal that leaves their friend and mentor dead. One year later, they regroup and come up with a daring plan to exact revenge on the traitor and reclaim their ill-gotten gains. Crime thriller inspired by the 1969 movie, starring Mark Wahlberg, Charlize Theron, Edward Norton, Seth Green and Donald Sutherland

programme.desc: The latest news from the world of entertainment.

Robbie Coltrane and Art Malik guest star, with George Cole and Patrick Malahide

programme.title: French Open Tennis: Roland-Garros Live
programme.desc: Coverage of the 12th day's play at Roland-Garros, featuring the women's singles semi-finals in the Grand Slam tournament. John Inverdale presents, with analysis from Jim Courier, Fabrice Santoro, Marion Bartoli, Mark Petchey, Anne Keothavong and Sam Smith, commentary by Nick Mullins and Jonathan Overend, and reports from Celina Hinchcliffe

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as one-time Coronation Street actor Jimi Mistry (Khalid Nazir), former Emmerdale star Gemma Atkinson (Carly Hope), Loose Women's Nadia Sawalha and veteran actor James Cosmo pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser, in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds for the charity of their choice. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: World Rally Championship Highlights
programme.desc: The fifth round of the season saw the action head to Italy, where rugged and sun-baked gravel tracks in the Mediterranean island of Sardinia ensured one of the season's sternest challenges. Fast and narrow stages left no margin for error, while intense heat combined with abrasive roads to test tyres to the limit.

programme.title: French Open Tennis: Roland-Garros Live
programme.desc: Coverage of the 13th day's play at Roland-Garros, featuring the men's singles semi-finals in the Grand Slam tournament. Not for the first time, Rafael Nadal reached last year's final without losing a set throughout the tournament, overcoming Diego Schwartzman 6-3, 6-3, 7-6 in the last four. It was Novak Djokovic who joined him in the showpiece match, but it took him five sets to eventually defeat Stefanos Tsitsipas. John Inverdale presents, with analysis from Jim Courier, Fabrice Santoro, Marion Bartoli, Mark Petchey, Anne Keothavong and Sam Smith, commentary by Nick Mullins and Jonathan Overend, and reports from Celina Hinchcliffe
